content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/felt/.claude/skills/surf

# Surf Browser Automation

Control Chrome browser via CLI or Unix socket.

## CLI Quick Reference

```bash
surf --help                    # Full help
surf <group>                   # Group help (tab, scroll, page, wait, dialog, emulate, form, perf, ai)
surf --help-full               # All commands
surf --find <term>             # Search tools
surf --help-topic <topic>      # Topic guide (refs, semantic, frames, devices, windows)
```

## Core Workflow

```bash
# 1. Navigate to page
surf navigate "https://example.com"

# 2. Read page to get element refs
surf page.read

# 3. Click by ref or coordinates
surf click --ref "e1"
surf click --x 100 --y 200

# 4. Type text
surf type --text "hello"

# 5. Screenshot
surf screenshot --output /tmp/shot.png
```

## AI Assistants (No API Keys)

Query AI models using your browser's logged-in session. Must be logged into the respective service in Chrome.

### ChatGPT
```bash
surf chatgpt "explain this code"
surf chatgpt "summarize" --with-page              # Include current page context
surf chatgpt "review" --model gpt-4o              # Specify model
surf chatgpt "analyze" --file document.pdf        # With file attachment
```

### Gemini
```bash
surf gemini "explain quantum computing"
surf gemini "summarize" --with-page               # Include page context
surf gemini "analyze" --file data.csv             # Attach file
surf gemini "a robot surfing" --generate-image /tmp/robot.png
surf gemini "add sunglasses" --edit-image photo.jpg --output out.jpg
surf gemini "summarize" --youtube "https://youtube.com/..."
surf gemini "hello" --model gemini-2.5-flash      # Models: gemini-3-pro (default), gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash
surf gemini "wide banner" --generate-image /tmp/banner.png --aspect-ratio 16:9
```

### Perplexity
```bash
surf perplexity "what is quantum computing"
surf perplexity "explain this page" --with-page   # Include page context
surf perplexity "deep dive" --mode research       # Research mode (Pro)
surf perplexity "latest news" --model sonar       # Model selection (Pro)
```

### Grok (via x.com - requires X.com login in Chrome)
```bash
surf grok "what are the latest AI trends on X"    # Search X posts
surf grok "analyze @username recent activity"     # Profile analysis  
surf grok "summarize this page" --with-page       # Include page context
surf grok "find viral AI posts" --deep-search     # DeepSearch mode
surf grok "quick question" --model fast           # Models: auto, fast, expert, thinking (default)
```

**Grok Validation & Troubleshooting:**
```bash
# Validate Grok UI and check available models (no query sent)
surf grok --validate

# If models changed, save discovered models to surf.json config
surf grok --validate --save-models
```

### AI Studio (via aistudio.google.com - requires Google login in Chrome)
```bash
surf aistudio "explain quantum computing"
surf aistudio "redteam this" --with-page          # Include current page context
surf aistudio "quick answer" --model gemini-3-flash-preview  # Model selection
surf aistudio "analyze" --timeout 600             # Custom timeout (default: 300s)
```

**Why AI Studio over Gemini?** AI Studio gives access to less restricted Gemini models. For Gemini 3 Pro the difference can be significant with certain prompts. Downside: aggressive per-day rate limits on Pro and Flash models.

**Model selection is best-effort:** Pass any AI Studio model id (e.g. `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, `gemini-3-flash-preview`, `gemini-flash-lite-latest`). If the model isn't found, AI Studio uses whatever model was last selected in the UI.

### AI Studio App Builder
```bash
surf aistudio.build "build a portfolio site"
surf aistudio.build "todo app" --model gemini-3.1-pro-preview   # Model override
surf aistudio.build "crm dashboard" --output ./out              # Extract zip to directory
surf aistudio.build "game" --keep-open --timeout 600            # Keep tab open, 10min timeout
```

Automates AI Studio's App Builder at `aistudio.google.com/apps`. Types your prompt, clicks Build, waits for completion, downloads the generated zip, and optionally extracts it.

- `--output <dir>` extracts the zip to a directory
- `--model <id>` overrides the model in Advanced Settings
- `--timeout <seconds>` build timeout (default: 600s)
- `--keep-open` leaves the AI Studio tab open after completion

Returns `zipPath`, `extractedPath`, `model`, `buildDuration`, and `tookMs`.

### AI Tool Troubleshooting

When AI queries fail, check these common issues:

1. **Not logged in**: The error "login required" means you need to log into the service in Chrome (chatgpt.com, gemini.google.com, perplexity.ai, x.com, or aistudio.google.com)
2. **Model selection failed**: The UI may have changed. Run `surf grok --validate` to check
3. **Response timeout**: Thinking models (ChatGPT o1, Grok thinking) can take 45+ seconds. AI Studio builds can take several minutes.
4. **Element not found**: The service's UI changed. Check for surf-cli updates

**Debugging workflow for agents:**
```bash
# 1. Check if the service is accessible and UI is valid
surf grok --validate

# 2. If models mismatch, update the local settings
surf grok --validate --save-models

# 3. Retry with explicit model name from validation output
surf grok "query" --model <model-from-validation>

# 4. If still failing, try with longer timeout
surf grok "query" --timeout 600
```

## Tab Management

```bash
surf tab.list
surf tab.new "https://google.com"
surf tab.switch 12345
surf tab.close 12345
surf tab.reload                # Reload current tab

# Named tabs (aliases)
surf tab.name myapp            # Name current tab
surf tab.switch myapp          # Switch by name
surf tab.named                 # List named tabs
surf tab.unname myapp          # Remove name

# Tab groups
surf tab.group                 # Create/add to tab group
surf tab.ungroup               # Remove from group
surf tab.groups                # List all tab groups
```

## Window Management

```bash
surf window.list                              # List all windows
surf window.list --tabs                       # Include tab details
surf window.new                               # New window
surf window.new --url "https://example.com"   # New window with URL
surf window.new --incognito                   # New incognito window
surf window.new --unfocused                   # Don't focus new window
surf window.focus 12345                       # Focus window by ID
surf window.close 12345                       # Close window
surf window.resize --id 123 --width 1920 --height 1080
surf window.resize --id 123 --state maximized # States: normal, minimized, maximized, fullscreen
```

**Window isolation for agents:**
```bash
# Create isolated window for agent work
surf window.new "https://example.com"
# Returns window ID, use with subsequent commands:
surf --window-id 123 tab.list
surf --window-id 123 go "https://other.com"
```

## Input Methods

```bash
# CDP method (real events) - default
surf type --text "hello"
surf click --x 100 --y 200

# JS method (DOM manipulation) - for contenteditable
surf type --text "hello" --selector "#input" --method js

# Keys
surf key Enter
surf key "cmd+a"
surf key.repeat --key Tab --count 5           # Repeat key presses

# Hover and drag
surf hover --ref e5
surf drag --from-x 100 --from-y 100 --to-x 200 --to-y 200
```

## Page Inspection

```bash
surf page.read                 # Accessibility tree with refs + page text
surf page.read --no-text       # Interactive elements only (no text content)
surf page.read --ref e5        # Get specific element details
surf page.read --depth 3       # Limit tree depth
surf page.read --compact       # Minimal output for LLM efficiency
surf page.text                 # Plain text content only
surf page.state                # Modals, loading state, scroll info
```

## Semantic Element Location

Find and act on elements by role, text, or label instead of refs:

```bash
# Find by ARIA role
surf locate.role button --name "Submit" --action click
surf locate.role textbox --name "Email" --action fill --value "test@example.com"
surf locate.role link --all                    # Return all matches

# Find by text content
surf locate.text "Sign In" --action click
surf locate.text "Accept" --exact --action click

# Find form field by label
surf locate.label "Username" --action fill --value "john"
surf locate.label "Password" --action fill --value "secret"
```

**Actions:** `click`, `fill`, `hover`, `text` (get text content)

## Text Search

```bash
surf search "login"                    # Find text in page
surf search "Error" --case-sensitive   # Case-sensitive
surf search "button" --limit 5         # Limit results
surf find "login"                      # Alias for search
```

## Element Inspection

```bash
surf element.styles e5                 # Get computed styles by ref
surf element.styles ".card"            # Or by CSS selector
# Returns: font, color, background, border, padding, bounding box
```

## Scrolling

```bash
surf scroll.bottom
surf scroll.top  
surf scroll.to --y 500         # Scroll to Y position
surf scroll.to --ref e5        # Scroll element into view
surf scroll.by --y 200         # Scroll by amount
surf scroll.info               # Get scroll position
```

## Waiting

```bash
surf wait 2                    # Wait 2 seconds
surf wait.element ".loaded"    # Wait for element
surf wait.network              # Wait for network idle
surf wait.url "/success"       # Wait for URL pattern
surf wait.dom --stable 100     # Wait for DOM stability
surf wait.load                 # Wait for page load complete
```

## Dialog Handling

```bash
surf dialog.info               # Get current dialog type/message
surf dialog.accept             # Accept (OK)
surf dialog.accept --text "response"  # Accept prompt with text
surf dialog.dismiss            # Dismiss (Cancel)
```

## Device/Network Emulation

```bash
# Network throttling
surf emulate.network slow-3g   # Presets: slow-3g, fast-3g, 4g, offline
surf emulate.network reset     # Disable throttling

# CPU throttling  
surf emulate.cpu 4             # 4x slower
surf emulate.cpu 1             # Reset

# Device emulation (19 presets)
surf emulate.device "iPhone 14"
surf emulate.device "Pixel 7"
surf emulate.device --list     # List available devices

# Custom viewport
surf emulate.viewport --width 1280 --height 720
surf emulate.touch --enable    # Enable touch emulation

# Geolocation
surf emulate.geo --lat 37.7749 --lon -122.4194
surf emulate.geo --clear
```

## Form Automation

```bash
surf page.read                 # Get element refs first

# Fill by ref
surf form.fill --data '[{"ref":"e1","value":"John"},{"ref":"e2","value":"john@example.com"}]'

# Checkboxes: true/false
surf form.fill --data '[{"ref":"e7","value":true}]'

# Dropdown selection
surf select e5 "Option A"                    # By value (default)
surf select e5 "Option A" "Option B"         # Multi-select
surf select e5 --by label "Display Text"     # By visible label
surf select e5 --by index 2                  # By index (0-based)
```

## File Upload

```bash
surf upload --ref e5 --files "/path/to/file.txt"
surf upload --ref e5 --files "/path/file1.txt,/path/file2.txt"
```

## Iframe Handling

```bash
surf frame.list                # List frames with IDs
surf frame.switch "FRAME_ID"   # Switch to iframe context
surf frame.main                # Return to main frame
surf frame.js --id "FRAME_ID" --code "return document.title"

# After frame.switch, subsequent commands target that frame:
surf frame.switch "iframe-1"
surf page.read                 # Reads iframe content
surf click e5                  # Clicks in iframe
surf frame.main                # Back to main page
```

## Network Inspection

```bash
surf network                   # List captured requests
surf network --stream          # Real-time network events
surf network.get --id "req-123"   # Full request details
surf network.body --id "req-123"  # Get response body
surf network.curl --id "req-123"  # Generate curl command
surf network.origins           # List origins with stats
surf network.stats             # Capture statistics
surf network.export            # Export all requests
surf network.clear             # Clear captured requests
```

## Console

```bash
surf console                   # Get console messages
surf console --stream          # Real-time console
surf console --stream --level error  # Errors only
```

## JavaScript Execution

```bash
surf js "return document.title"
surf js "document.querySelector('.btn').click()"
```

## Performance

```bash
surf perf.metrics              # Current metrics snapshot
surf perf.start                # Start trace
surf perf.stop                 # Stop and get results
```

## Screenshots

```bash
surf screenshot                           # Auto-saves to /tmp/surf-snap-*.png
surf screenshot --output /tmp/shot.png    # Save to specific file
surf screenshot --selector ".card"        # Element only
surf screenshot --full-page               # Full page scroll capture
surf screenshot --no-save                 # Return base64 only, don't save file
```

## Zoom

```bash
surf zoom                      # Get current zoom level
surf zoom 1.5                  # Set zoom to 150%
surf zoom 1                    # Reset to 100%
```

## Cookies & Storage

```bash
surf cookie.list               # List cookies for current page
surf cookie.list --domain .google.com
surf cookie.set --name "token" --value "abc123"
surf cookie.get --name "token"
surf cookie.clear              # Clear all cookies
```

## History & Bookmarks

```bash
surf history --query "github" --max 20
surf bookmarks --query "docs"
surf bookmark.add --url "https://..." --title "My Bookmark"
surf bookmark.remove
```

## Health Checks & Smoke Tests

```bash
surf health --url "http://localhost:3000"
surf smoke --urls "http://localhost:3000" "http://localhost:3000/about"
surf smoke --urls "..." --screenshot /tmp/smoke
```

## Workflows

Execute multi-step browser automation as a single command with smart auto-waits.

### Inline Workflows

```bash
# Pipe-separated commands
surf do 'go "https://example.com" | click e5 | screenshot'

# Multi-step login flow
surf do 'go "https://example.com/login" | type "user@example.com" --selector "#email" | type "pass" --selector "#password" | click --selector "button[type=submit]"'

# Validate without executing
surf do 'go "url" | click e5' --dry-run
```

### Named Workflows

Save workflows as JSON files in `~/.surf/workflows/` (user) or `./.surf/workflows/` (project):

```bash
# List available workflows
surf workflow.list

# Show workflow details
surf workflow.info my-workflow

# Run by name with arguments
surf do my-workflow --email "user@example.com" --password "secret"

# Validate workflow file
surf workflow.validate workflow.json
```

### Workflow JSON Format

```json
{
  "name": "Login Flow",
  "description": "Automate login process",
  "args": {
    "email": { "required": true },
    "password": { "required": true },
    "url": { "default": "https://example.com/login" }
  },
  "steps": [
    { "tool": "navigate", "args": { "url": "%{url}" } },
    { "tool": "type", "args": { "text": "%{email}", "selector": "input[name=email]" } },
    { "tool": "type", "args": { "text": "%{password}", "selector": "input[name=password]" } },
    { "tool": "click", "args": { "selector": "button[type=submit]" } },
    { "tool": "screenshot", "args": {}, "as": "result" }
  ]
}
```

### Loops and Step Outputs

```json
{
  "steps": [
    // Capture step output for later use
    { "tool": "js", "args": { "code": "return [1,2,3]" }, "as": "items" },
    
    // Fixed iterations
    { "repeat": 5, "steps": [
      { "tool": "click", "args": { "ref": "e5" } }
    ]},
    
    // Iterate over array
    { "each": "%{items}", "as": "item", "steps": [
      { "tool": "js", "args": { "code": "console.log('%{item}')" } }
    ]},
    
    // Repeat until condition
    { "repeat": 20, "until": { "tool": "js", "args": { "code": "return done" } }, "steps": [...] }
  ]
}
```

### Workflow Options

```bash
--file, -f <path>     # Load from JSON file
--dry-run             # Parse and validate without executing
--on-error stop|continue  # Error handling (default: stop)
--step-delay <ms>     # Delay between steps (default: 100, 0 to disable)
--no-auto-wait        # Disable automatic waits
--json                # Structured JSON output
```

**Auto-waits:** Commands automatically wait for completion:
- Navigation (`go`, `back`, `forward`) → waits for page load
- Clicks, key presses, form fills → waits for DOM stability
- Tab switches → waits for tab to load

**Why use `do`?** Instead of 6-8 separate CLI calls with LLM orchestration between each, a workflow executes deterministically. Faster, cheaper, and more reliable.

## Error Diagnostics

```bash
# Auto-capture screenshot + console on failure
surf wait.element ".missing" --auto-capture --timeout 2000
# Saves to /tmp/surf-error-*.png
```

## Common Options

```bash
--tab-id <id>         # Target specific tab
--window-id <id>      # Target specific window
--json                # Raw JSON output  
--auto-capture        # Screenshot + console on error
--timeout <ms>        # Override default timeout
```

## Tips

1. **First CDP operation is slow** (~5-8s) - debugger attachment overhead, subsequent calls fast
2. **Use refs from page.read** for reliable element targeting over CSS selectors
3. **JS method for contenteditable** - Modern editors (ChatGPT, Claude, Notion) need `--method js`
4. **Named tabs for workflows** - `tab.name app` then `tab.switch app`
5. **Auto-capture for debugging** - `--auto-capture` saves diagnostics on failure
6. **AI tools use browser session** - Must be logged into the service (ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, Grok, AI Studio), no API keys needed
7. **Grok validation** - Run `surf grok --validate` if queries fail to check UI changes
8. **Long timeouts for thinking models** - ChatGPT o1, Grok thinking can take 60+ seconds. AI Studio builds default to 600s.
9. **AI Studio for unrestricted Gemini** - `surf aistudio` gives less filtered responses than `surf gemini` for the same models
10. **Use `surf do` for multi-step tasks** - Reduces token overhead and improves reliability
11. **Dry-run workflows first** - `surf do '...' --dry-run` validates without executing
12. **Window isolation** - Use `window.new` + `--window-id` to keep agent work separate from your browsing
13. **Semantic locators** - `locate.role`, `locate.text`, `locate.label` for more robust element finding
14. **Frame context** - Use `frame.switch` before interacting with iframe content

## Socket API

For programmatic access:

```bash
echo '{"type":"tool_request","method":"execute_tool","params":{"tool":"tab.list","args":{}},"id":"1"}' | nc -U /tmp/surf.sock
```

     8→  type TypedData,
     9→  SignerInterface,
    10→  hash,
    11→  transaction,
    12→  CallData,
    13→  EDAMode,
    14→  type EDataAvailabilityMode,
    15→  typedData as typedDataUtils,
    16→} from "starknet"
    17→import { FalconService } from "./FalconService"
    18→import { TransactionSignError } from "./errors"
    19→
    20→function intDAM(dam: EDataAvailabilityMode): EDAMode {
    21→  if (dam === "L1") return EDAMode.L1
    22→  if (dam === "L2") return EDAMode.L2
    23→  throw new Error(`Unknown EDataAvailabilityMode: ${dam}`)
    24→}
    25→
    26→export class FalconSigner extends SignerInterface {
    27→  constructor(
    28→    private readonly sk: Uint8Array,
    29→    private readonly pkNtt: Int32Array,
    30→    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    31→    private readonly runtime: ManagedRuntime.ManagedRuntime<FalconService, any>,
    32→  ) {
    33→    super()
    34→  }
    35→
    36→  async getPubKey(): Promise<string> {
    37→    // Falcon public keys are not a single felt — return a placeholder.
    38→    // The actual PK is the 29 packed felt252 slots stored in the contract.
    39→    return "0x0"
    40→  }
    41→
    42→  async signMessage(
    43→    typedDataObj: TypedData,
    44→    accountAddress: string,
    45→  ): Promise<Signature> {
    46→    const msgHash = typedDataUtils.getMessageHash(typedDataObj, accountAddress)
    47→    return this._signHash(msgHash)
    48→  }
    49→
    50→  async signTransaction(
    51→    transactions: Call[],
    52→    transactionsDetail: InvocationsSignerDetails,
    53→  ): Promise<Signature> {
    54→    const compiledCalldata = transaction.getExecuteCalldata(
    55→      transactions,
    56→      transactionsDetail.cairoVersion,
    57→    )
    58→    const txHash = hash.calculateInvokeTransactionHash({
    59→      ...transactionsDetail,
    60→      senderAddress: transactionsDetail.walletAddress,
    61→      compiledCalldata,
    62→      version: transactionsDetail.version,
    63→      nonceDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(transactionsDetail.nonceDataAvailabilityMode),
    64→      feeDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(transactionsDetail.feeDataAvailabilityMode),
    65→    })
    66→    return this._signHash(txHash)
    67→  }
    68→
    69→  async signDeployAccountTransaction(
    70→    details: DeployAccountSignerDetails,
    71→  ): Promise<Signature> {
    72→    const compiledConstructorCalldata = CallData.compile(
    73→      details.constructorCalldata,
    74→    )
    75→    const txHash = hash.calculateDeployAccountTransactionHash({
    76→      ...details,
    77→      salt: details.addressSalt,
    78→      compiledConstructorCalldata,
    79→      version: details.version,
    80→      nonceDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(details.nonceDataAvailabilityMode),
    81→      feeDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(details.feeDataAvailabilityMode),
    82→    })
    83→    return this._signHash(txHash)
    84→  }
    85→
    86→  async signDeclareTransaction(
    87→    details: DeclareSignerDetails,
    88→  ): Promise<Signature> {
    89→    const txHash = hash.calculateDeclareTransactionHash({
    90→      ...details,
    91→      version: details.version,
    92→      nonceDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(details.nonceDataAvailabilityMode),
    93→      feeDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(details.feeDataAvailabilityMode),
    94→    })
    95→    return this._signHash(txHash)
    96→  }
    97→
    98→  private async _signHash(txHash: string): Promise<string[]> {
    99→    const exit = await this.runtime.runPromiseExit(
   100→      FalconService.signForStarknet(this.sk, txHash, this.pkNtt),
   101→    )
   102→
   103→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
   104→      return exit.value
   105→    }
   106→
   107→    const errOpt = Cause.failureOption(exit.cause)
   108→    const msg = Option.match(errOpt, {
   109→      onNone: () => "Falcon signing failed",
   110→      onSome: (e) => e.message,
   111→    })
   112→    throw new TransactionSignError({ message: msg })
   113→  }
   114→}
   115→

1→TARGET_DIR = target
     2→REDACTED
     3→
     4→install-stwo-run-and-prove:
     5→	cargo +nightly-2025-07-14 install \
     6→		--git ssh://git@github.com/m-kus/proving-utils.git \
     7→		--rev $(PROVING_UTILS_REV) \
     8→		stwo_run_and_prove --force
     9→
    10→falcon-execute:
    11→	rm -rf $(TARGET_DIR)/execute/falcon \
    12→		&& cd packages/falcon \
    13→		&& scarb execute --arguments-file tests/data/args_512_1.json --print-resource-usage --save-profiler-trace-data
    14→
    15→falcon-args:
    16→	python packages/falcon/scripts/generate_args.py --n 512 --num_signatures 1 > packages/falcon/tests/data/args_512_1.json
    17→	python packages/falcon/scripts/generate_args.py --n 1024 --num_signatures 1 > packages/falcon/tests/data/args_1024_1.json
    18→
    19→falcon-build:
    20→	scarb --profile release build --package falcon
    21→
    22→falcon-prove:
    23→	stwo_run_and_prove \
    24→		--program resources/simple_bootloader_compiled.json \
    25→		--program_input packages/falcon/proving_task.json \
    26→		--prover_params_json prover_params.json \
    27→		--proofs_dir $(TARGET_DIR) \
    28→		--proof-format cairo-serde \
    29→		--verify
    30→
    31→falcon-burn:
    32→	scarb burn --package falcon \
    33→		--arguments-file packages/falcon/tests/data/args_512_1.json \
    34→		--output-file target/falcon.svg \
    35→		--open-in-browser
    36→
    37→regenerate-ntt:
    38→	python3 -m cairo_gen.circuits.regenerate ntt --n 512
    39→
    40→regenerate-intt:
    41→	python3 -m cairo_gen.circuits.regenerate intt --n 512
    42→
    43→regenerate-all:
    44→	python3 -m cairo_gen.circuits.regenerate all --n 512
    45→
    46→FALCON_RS_DIR = ../falcon-rs
    47→WASM_OUT_DIR = apps/demo/public/wasm
    48→
    49→wasm-build:
    50→	cd $(FALCON_RS_DIR) && wasm-pack build --target web --features wasm --no-default-features
    51→	cp $(FALCON_RS_DIR)/pkg/falcon_rs_bg.wasm $(WASM_OUT_DIR)/falcon_rs_bg.wasm
    52→	cp $(FALCON_RS_DIR)/pkg/falcon_rs.js $(WASM_OUT_DIR)/falcon_rs.js
    53→	cp $(FALCON_RS_DIR)/pkg/falcon_rs.d.ts $(WASM_OUT_DIR)/falcon_rs.d.ts
    54→	cp $(FALCON_RS_DIR)/pkg/falcon_rs_bg.wasm.d.ts $(WASM_OUT_DIR)/falcon_rs_bg.wasm.d.ts
    55→	@echo "WASM built and copied to $(WASM_OUT_DIR)"
    56→	@ls -lh $(WASM_OUT_DIR)/falcon_rs_bg.wasm
    57→
    58→DEMO_PORT = 3737
    59→
    60→demo-serve:
    61→	@if lsof -ti :$(DEMO_PORT) >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
    62→		echo "Port $(DEMO_PORT) already in use"; exit 1; \
    63→	fi
    64→	cd apps/demo && npx next dev --turbopack -H 0.0.0.0 -p $(DEMO_PORT)
    65→
    66→demo-stop:
    67→	@lsof -ti :$(DEMO_PORT) | xargs -r kill && echo "Demo stopped" || echo "Nothing on port $(DEMO_PORT)"
    68→

1→import { Effect } from "effect"
     2→import { WasmRuntime } from "./WasmRuntime"
     3→import {
     4→  KeygenError,
     5→  SigningError,
     6→  HintGenerationError,
     7→  PackingError,
     8→  VerificationError,
     9→} from "./errors"
    10→import type {
    11→  FalconKeypair,
    12→  FalconSignatureResult,
    13→  PackedPublicKey,
    14→} from "./types"
    15→
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→// VK byte deserialization
    18→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    19→
    20→/**
    21→ * Deserialize a Falcon-512 verifying key (896 bytes) into 512 NTT-domain
    22→ * coefficients stored as an Int32Array.
    23→ *
    24→ * Format (falcon-rs, PUBLIC_KEY_LEN = 896, no header):
    25→ *   - 512 coefficients x 14 bits each, packed LSB-first
    26→ *
    27→ * Algorithm: maintains a bit-buffer, loading bytes from the input one at a
    28→ * time and extracting 14-bit windows.
    29→ *
    30→ * Throws on invalid input (too few bytes, or any coefficient >= Q=12289).
    31→ */
    32→function parsePublicKeyNttBytes(vkBytes: Uint8Array): Int32Array {
    33→  const Q = 12289
    34→  const N = 512
    35→  const BITS_PER_COEFF = 14
    36→  const DATA_BYTES = (N * BITS_PER_COEFF) / 8 // 896
    37→
    38→  if (vkBytes.length < DATA_BYTES) {
    39→    throw new Error(
    40→      `VK too short: expected at least ${DATA_BYTES} bytes, got ${vkBytes.length}`,
    41→    )
    42→  }
    43→
    44→  const coeffs = new Int32Array(N)
    45→  const mask = (1 << BITS_PER_COEFF) - 1 // 0x3FFF
    46→
    47→  let bitBuffer = 0
    48→  let bitsInBuffer = 0
    49→  let byteIndex = 0
    50→
    51→  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
    52→    while (bitsInBuffer < BITS_PER_COEFF) {
    53→      bitBuffer |= vkBytes[byteIndex++] << bitsInBuffer
    54→      bitsInBuffer += 8
    55→    }
    56→    const coeff = bitBuffer & mask
    57→    bitBuffer >>= BITS_PER_COEFF
    58→    bitsInBuffer -= BITS_PER_COEFF
    59→
    60→    if (coeff >= Q) {
    61→      throw new Error(
    62→        `Invalid VK coefficient ${coeff} at index ${i} (must be < ${Q})`,
    63→      )
    64→    }
    65→    coeffs[i] = coeff
    66→  }
    67→
    68→  return coeffs
    69→}
    70→
    71→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    72→// FalconService
    73→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    74→
    75→export class FalconService extends Effect.Service<FalconService>()(
    76→  "FalconService",
    77→  {
    78→    accessors: true,
    79→    // No pre-bundled dependencies: callers must provide WasmRuntime via Layer.
    80→    // This enables unit tests to inject a mock WasmRuntime without the live
    81→    // loader trying to fetch /wasm/falcon_rs.js at import time.
    82→    effect: Effect.gen(function* () {
    83→      const wasm = yield* WasmRuntime
    84→
    85→      const generateKeypair = Effect.fn("Falcon.generateKeypair")(
    86→        function* (seed?: Uint8Array) {
    87→          const s =
    88→            seed ?? crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(32))
    89→          const result = yield* Effect.try({
    90→            try: () => wasm.keygen(s),
    91→            catch: (error) =>
    92→              new KeygenError({ message: String(error) }),
    93→          })
    94→          const publicKeyNtt = yield* Effect.try({
    95→            try: () => parsePublicKeyNttBytes(result.vk),
    96→            catch: (error) =>
    97→              new KeygenError({ message: String(error) }),
    98→          })
    99→          return {
   100→            secretKey: result.sk,
   101→            verifyingKey: result.vk,
   102→            publicKeyNtt,
   103→          } satisfies FalconKeypair
   104→        },
   105→      )
   106→
   107→      const sign = Effect.fn("Falcon.sign")(
   108→        function* (
   109→          secretKey: Uint8Array,
   110→          message: Uint8Array,
   111→        ) {
   112→          const result = yield* Effect.try({
   113→            try: () =>
   114→              wasm.sign(secretKey, message, crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(40))),
   115→            catch: (error) =>
   116→              new SigningError({ message: String(error) }),
   117→          })
   118→          return result satisfies FalconSignatureResult
   119→        },
   120→      )
   121→
   122→      const verify = Effect.fn("Falcon.verify")(
   123→        function* (
   124→          verifyingKey: Uint8Array,
   125→          message: Uint8Array,
   126→          signature: Uint8Array,
   127→        ) {
   128→          return yield* Effect.try({
   129→            try: () => wasm.verify(verifyingKey, message, signature),
   130→            catch: (error) =>
   131→              new VerificationError({
   132→                message: String(error),
   133→                step: "verify",
   134→              }),
   135→          })
   136→        },
   137→      )
   138→
   139→      const createHint = Effect.fn("Falcon.createHint")(
   140→        function* (
   141→          s1: Int32Array,
   142→          pkNtt: Int32Array,
   143→        ) {
   144→          return yield* Effect.try({
   145→            try: () => wasm.create_verification_hint(s1, pkNtt),
   146→            catch: (error) =>
   147→              new HintGenerationError({ message: String(error) }),
   148→          })
   149→        },
   150→      )
   151→
   152→      const packPublicKey = Effect.fn("Falcon.packPublicKey")(
   153→        function* (
   154→          pkNtt: Uint16Array,
   155→        ) {
   156→          const slots = yield* Effect.try({
   157→            try: () => wasm.pack_public_key_wasm(pkNtt),
   158→            catch: (error) =>
   159→              new PackingError({ message: String(error) }),
   160→          })
   161→          return { slots } satisfies PackedPublicKey
   162→        },
   163→      )
   164→
   165→      const deserializePublicKeyNtt = Effect.fn(
   166→        "Falcon.deserializePublicKeyNtt",
   167→      )(function* (vkBytes: Uint8Array) {
   168→        return yield* Effect.try({
   169→          try: () => parsePublicKeyNttBytes(vkBytes),
   170→          catch: (error) =>
   171→            new KeygenError({ message: String(error) }),
   172→        })
   173→      })
   174→
   175→      const signForStarknet = Effect.fn("Falcon.signForStarknet")(
   176→        function* (
   177→          secretKey: Uint8Array,
   178→          txHash: string,
   179→          pkNtt: Int32Array,
   180→        ) {
   181→          return yield* Effect.try({
   182→            try: () => wasm.sign_for_starknet(secretKey, txHash, pkNtt),
   183→            catch: (error) =>
   184→              new SigningError({ message: String(error) }),
   185→          })
   186→        },
   187→      )
   188→
   189→      return {
   190→        generateKeypair,
   191→        sign,
   192→        verify,
   193→        createHint,
   194→        packPublicKey,
   195→        deserializePublicKeyNtt,
   196→        signForStarknet,
   197→      }
   198→    }),
   199→  },
   200→) {}
   201→

content.content:
1→import { Config, Effect, Layer } from "effect"
     2→import { RpcProvider, Account, hash, stark } from "starknet"
     3→import type { SignerInterface } from "starknet"
     4→import {
     5→  StarknetRpcError,
     6→  AccountDeployError,
     7→  DevnetFetchError,
     8→  TransactionSubmitError,
     9→} from "./errors"
    10→import { TxHash, ContractAddress } from "./types"
    11→import type { PackedPublicKey, DevnetAccount } from "./types"
    12→
    13→/** STRK token contract address (same on mainnet and Sepolia) */
    14→export const STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS =
    15→  "0x04718f5a0fc34cc1af16a1cdee98ffb20c31f5cd61d6ab07201858f4287c938d"
    16→
    17→export function parsePrefundedAccounts(raw: unknown[]): DevnetAccount[] {
    18→  return raw
    19→    .filter((item): item is Record<string, string> =>
    20→      typeof item === "object" && item !== null &&
    21→      "address" in item && "private_key" in item
    22→    )
    23→    .map((item) => ({
    24→      address: item.address,
    25→      private_key: item.private_key,
    26→      initial_balance: item.initial_balance ?? "0",
    27→    }))
    28→}
    29→
    30→function makeService(rpcUrl: string, classHash: string) {
    31→  const provider = new RpcProvider({ nodeUrl: rpcUrl })
    32→
    33→  const computeDeployAddress = Effect.fn("Starknet.computeDeployAddress")(
    34→    function* (packedPk: PackedPublicKey) {
    35→      // PackedPolynomial512 is a flat struct (s0..s28), not an Array.
    36→      // CallData.compile would add a length prefix; pass raw slots instead.
    37→      const constructorCalldata = [...packedPk.slots]
    38→      const salt = stark.randomAddress()
    39→      const address = hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(
    40→        salt,
    41→        classHash,
    42→        constructorCalldata,
    43→        0,
    44→      )
    45→      return {
    46→        address: ContractAddress.make(address),
    47→        salt,
    48→      }
    49→    },
    50→  )
    51→
    52→  const getBalance = Effect.fn("Starknet.getBalance")(
    53→    function* (address: string) {
    54→      return yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    55→        try: async () => {
    56→          const result = await provider.callContract({
    57→            contractAddress: STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS,
    58→            entrypoint: "balanceOf",
    59→            calldata: [address],
    60→          })
    61→          return BigInt(result[0])
    62→        },
    63→        catch: (error) =>
    64→          new StarknetRpcError({ message: String(error), code: -1 }),
    65→      })
    66→    },
    67→  )
    68→
    69→  const deployAccount = Effect.fn("Starknet.deployAccount")(
    70→    function* (
    71→      packedPk: PackedPublicKey,
    72→      signer: SignerInterface,
    73→      salt: string,
    74→    ) {
    75→      // PackedPolynomial512 is a flat struct (s0..s28), not an Array.
    76→      // CallData.compile would add a length prefix; pass raw slots instead.
    77→      const constructorCalldata = [...packedPk.slots]
    78→      const address = hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(
    79→        salt,
    80→        classHash,
    81→        constructorCalldata,
    82→        0,
    83→      )
    84→
    85→      const account = new Account({ provider, address, signer })
    86→
    87→      const result = yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    88→        try: () =>
    89→          account.deployAccount({
    90→            classHash,
    91→            constructorCalldata,
    92→            addressSalt: salt,
    93→            contractAddress: address,
    94→          }),
    95→        catch: (error) =>
    96→          new AccountDeployError({ message: String(error) }),
    97→      })
    98→
    99→      yield* Effect.tryPromise({
   100→        try: () => provider.waitForTransaction(result.transaction_hash),
   101→        catch: (error) =>
   102→          new AccountDeployError({
   103→            message: `Tx failed: ${error}`,
   104→            txHash: result.transaction_hash,
   105→          }),
   106→      })
   107→
   108→      return {
   109→        txHash: TxHash.make(result.transaction_hash),
   110→        address: ContractAddress.make(result.contract_address),
   111→      }
   112→    },
   113→  )
   114→
   115→  const waitForTx = Effect.fn("Starknet.waitForTx")(
   116→    function* (txHash: string) {
   117→      yield* Effect.tryPromise({
   118→        try: () => provider.waitForTransaction(txHash),
   119→        catch: (error) =>
   120→          new StarknetRpcError({ message: String(error), code: -1 }),
   121→      })
   122→    },
   123→  )
   124→
   125→  const fetchPrefundedAccounts = Effect.fn("Starknet.fetchPrefundedAccounts")(
   126→    function* () {
   127→      return yield* Effect.tryPromise({
   128→        try: async () => {
   129→          const baseUrl = rpcUrl.replace(/\/rpc.*$/, "").replace(/\/$/, "")
   130→          const response = await fetch(baseUrl, {
   131→            method: "POST",
   132→            headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
   133→            body: JSON.stringify({
   134→              jsonrpc: "2.0",
   135→              id: 1,
   136→              method: "devnet_getPredeployedAccounts",
   137→              params: [],
   138→            }),
   139→          })
   140→          if (!response.ok) {
   141→            throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}`)
   142→          }
   143→          const json = await response.json()
   144→          if (json.error) {
   145→            throw new Error(json.error.message ?? JSON.stringify(json.error))
   146→          }
   147→          return parsePrefundedAccounts(json.result as unknown[])
   148→        },
   149→        catch: (error) =>
   150→          new DevnetFetchError({ message: `Failed to fetch prefunded accounts: ${error}` }),
   151→      })
   152→    },
   153→  )
   154→
   155→  const sendTransaction = Effect.fn("Starknet.sendTransaction")(
   156→    function* (
   157→      accountAddress: string,
   158→      signer: SignerInterface,
   159→      recipient: string,
   160→      amount: bigint,
   161→    ) {
   162→      const account = new Account({ provider, address: accountAddress, signer })
   163→      return yield* Effect.tryPromise({
   164→        try: async () => {
   165→          const result = await account.execute([
   166→            {
   167→              contractAddress: STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS,
   168→              entrypoint: "transfer",
   169→              calldata: [recipient, amount.toString(), "0"],
   170→            },
   171→          ])
   172→          await provider.waitForTransaction(result.transaction_hash)
   173→          return { txHash: TxHash.make(result.transaction_hash) }
   174→        },
   175→        catch: (error) =>
   176→          new TransactionSubmitError({ message: String(error) }),
   177→      })
   178→    },
   179→  )
   180→
   181→  return {
   182→    computeDeployAddress,
   183→    getBalance,
   184→    deployAccount,
   185→    waitForTx,
   186→    fetchPrefundedAccounts,
   187→    sendTransaction,
   188→    provider,
   189→  }
   190→}
   191→
   192→// @ts-ignore — StarknetService.make() intentionally overrides the inherited Effect.Service.make() with a Layer factory
   193→export class StarknetService extends Effect.Service<StarknetService>()(
   194→  "StarknetService",
   195→  {
   196→    accessors: true,
   197→    effect: Effect.gen(function* () {
   198→      const rpcUrl = yield* Config.string("NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL")
   199→      return makeService(rpcUrl, "0x0")
   200→    }),
   201→  },
   202→) {
   203→  /**
   204→   * Custom layer factory for runtime network switching.
   205→   * Use this instead of Default when you need a specific rpcUrl and classHash
   206→   * (e.g., switching between Sepolia and Mainnet at runtime).
   207→   */
   208→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
   209→  static make(rpcUrl: string, classHash: string): Layer.Layer<StarknetService> {
   210→    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
   211→    return Layer.succeed(StarknetService, makeService(rpcUrl, classHash) as any)
   212→  }
   213→}
   214→

1→import { Context, Effect, Layer } from "effect"
     2→import { WasmLoadError } from "./errors"
     3→
     4→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     5→// WasmModule interface
     6→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→
     8→export interface WasmModule {
     9→  readonly keygen: (seed: Uint8Array) => { sk: Uint8Array; vk: Uint8Array }
    10→  readonly sign: (
    11→    skBytes: Uint8Array,
    12→    message: Uint8Array,
    13→    salt: Uint8Array,
    14→  ) => {
    15→    signature: Uint8Array
    16→    salt: Uint8Array
    17→  }
    18→  readonly verify: (
    19→    vkBytes: Uint8Array,
    20→    message: Uint8Array,
    21→    signature: Uint8Array,
    22→  ) => boolean
    23→  readonly create_verification_hint: (
    24→    s1: Int32Array,
    25→    pkNtt: Int32Array,
    26→  ) => Uint16Array
    27→  readonly pack_public_key_wasm: (pkNtt: Uint16Array) => string[]
    28→  readonly public_key_length: () => number
    29→  readonly salt_length: () => number
    30→  readonly sign_for_starknet: (
    31→    skBytes: Uint8Array,
    32→    txHash: string,
    33→    pkNtt: Int32Array,
    34→  ) => string[]
    35→}
    36→
    37→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    38→// Runtime type validation — no `as any` or `as unknown as WasmModule`
    39→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    40→
    41→/**
    42→ * Runtime guard that checks every method in the WasmModule interface exists
    43→ * and is a function. Returns true only when all seven methods are present.
    44→ *
    45→ * This replaces the unsafe `as unknown as WasmModule` cast that was used in
    46→ * the original implementation.
    47→ *
    48→ * Validation scope: checks that each required key is present and is a
    49→ * function. Function arity and return types cannot be checked without
    50→ * invoking the function (which would have side effects), so only presence
    51→ * and kind are validated here.
    52→ *
    53→ * No type assertions are used: after the null/typeof guard, TypeScript
    54→ * narrows `mod` to `object`, which satisfies `Reflect.get`'s `target:
    55→ * object` parameter — property access is type-safe without any cast.
    56→ */
    57→export function isValidWasmModule(mod: unknown): mod is WasmModule {
    58→  if (mod === null || typeof mod !== "object") return false
    59→  // TypeScript narrows `mod` to `object` here — no type assertion needed.
    60→  // Reflect.get accepts `object` as its first argument directly.
    61→  const methods = [
    62→    "keygen",
    63→    "sign",
    64→    "verify",
    65→    "create_verification_hint",
    66→    "pack_public_key_wasm",
    67→    "public_key_length",
    68→    "salt_length",
    69→    "sign_for_starknet",
    70→  ] as const
    71→  return methods.every((method) => typeof Reflect.get(mod, method) === "function")
    72→}
    73→
    74→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75→// Context.Tag — WasmRuntime service tag
    76→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    77→
    78→export class WasmRuntime extends Context.Tag("WasmRuntime")<
    79→  WasmRuntime,
    80→  WasmModule
    81→>() {}
    82→
    83→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    84→// Internal load function — uses Effect.fn for automatic span tracing
    85→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    86→
    87→const loadWasmModule = Effect.fn("WasmRuntime.load")(function* () {
    88→  const mod = yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    89→    try: async () => {
    90→      // Dynamic import of the WASM JS glue generated by wasm-pack.
    91→      // The `/* webpackIgnore: true */` comment prevents bundlers from
    92→      // trying to resolve this path at build time.
    93→      const modulePath = "/wasm/falcon_rs.js"
    94→      const imported = await import(
    95→        /* webpackIgnore: true */
    96→        modulePath
    97→      )
    98→      // Run the WASM initializer (wasm-pack generates a default export
    99→      // that downloads and instantiates the .wasm binary).
   100→      await imported.default()
   101→      return imported
   102→    },
   103→    catch: (error) =>
   104→      new WasmLoadError({
   105→        message: `Failed to load WASM module: ${String(error)}`,
   106→      }),
   107→  })
   108→
   109→  // Runtime validation — replaces unsafe `as unknown as WasmModule`
   110→  if (!isValidWasmModule(mod)) {
   111→    return yield* Effect.fail(
   112→      new WasmLoadError({
   113→        message:
   114→          "WASM module is missing required methods. Expected: keygen, sign, verify, " +
   115→          "create_verification_hint, pack_public_key_wasm, public_key_length, salt_length, sign_for_starknet.",
   116→      }),
   117→    )
   118→  }
   119→
   120→  yield* Effect.log("WasmRuntime loaded successfully")
   121→  return mod
   122→})
   123→
   124→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   125→// WasmRuntimeLive — Layer that loads and validates the WASM module
   126→//
   127→// Effect's Layer memoization guarantees this effect runs at most once when
   128→// the Layer is provided to the application. There is no mutable global state
   129→// (`let cachedModule`); the singleton guarantee comes from Layer itself.
   130→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   131→
   132→export const WasmRuntimeLive = Layer.effect(WasmRuntime, loadWasmModule())
   133→

1→"use client" 2→ 3→import React, { useCallback, useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useState } from "react" 4→import { useAtomSet, useAtomValue } from "@effect-atom/atom-react" 5→import { Cause, Exit, Layer, ManagedRuntime, Option } from "effect" 6→import { keypairAtom } from "@/atoms/falcon" 7→import { 8→ deployStepAtom, 9→ deployedAddressAtom, 10→ deployTxHashAtom, 11→ networkAtom, 12→} from "@/atoms/starknet" 13→import { NETWORKS } from "@/config/networks" 14→import type { NetworkConfig } from "@/config/networks" 15→import { FalconService } from "@/services/FalconService" 16→import { FalconSigner } from "@/services/FalconSigner" 17→import { StarknetService } from "@/services/StarknetService" 18→import { WasmRuntimeLive } from "@/services/WasmRuntime" 19→import type { DevnetAccount 1→import { Effect, Option, Schema } from "effect"
     2→import type { SignerInterface } from "starknet"
     3→import { FalconService } from "@/services/FalconService"
     4→import { StarknetService } from "@/services/StarknetService"
     5→import { InsufficientFundsError } from "@/services/errors"
     6→import type {
     7→  ContractAddress,
     8→  FalconKeypair,
     9→  PackedPublicKey,
    10→} from "@/services/types"
    11→
    12→const FALCON_PUBLIC_KEY_LENGTH = 512
    13→const FALCON_MODULUS = 12289
    14→
    15→export class InvalidPrivateKeyError extends Schema.TaggedError<InvalidPrivateKeyError>()(
    16→  "InvalidPrivateKeyError",
    17→  { message: Schema.String },
    18→) {}
    19→
    20→export class InvalidPublicKeyNttError extends Schema.TaggedError<InvalidPublicKeyNttError>()(
    21→  "InvalidPublicKeyNttError",
    22→  { message: Schema.String },
    23→) {}
    24→
    25→export interface PreparedAccountDeploy {
    26→  readonly privateKey: string
    27→  readonly keypair: FalconKeypair
    28→  readonly packedPublicKey: PackedPublicKey
    29→  readonly address: ContractAddress
    30→  readonly salt: string
    31→}
    32→
    33→export interface DeployAccountInput {
    34→  readonly address: ContractAddress
    35→  readonly packedPublicKey: PackedPublicKey
    36→  readonly signer: SignerInterface
    37→  readonly salt: string
    38→  readonly requiredBalance: bigint
    39→}
    40→
    41→export const validateHexPrivateKey = Effect.fn(
    42→  "AccountDeployPipeline.validateHexPrivateKey",
    43→)((privateKey: string) =>
    44→  Effect.try({
    45→    try: () => {
    46→      const trimmed = privateKey.trim()
    47→      const normalized = trimmed.toLowerCase()
    48→
    49→      if (!normalized.startsWith("0x")) {
    50→        throw new Error("Private key must be 0x-prefixed")
    51→      }
    52→
    53→      const hexBody = normalized.slice(2)
    54→      if (hexBody.length === 0 || hexBody.length > 64) {
    55→        throw new Error("Private key must be between 1 and 32 bytes hex")
    56→      }
    57→
    58→      if (!/^[0-9a-f]+$/u.test(hexBody)) {
    59→        throw new Error("Private key contains non-hex characters")
    60→      }
    61→
    62→      // Pad to 32 bytes for consistency (Starknet private keys may be shorter)
    63→      return `0x${hexBody.padStart(64, "0")}`
    64→    },
    65→    catch: (error) =>
    66→      new InvalidPrivateKeyError({
    67→        message: String(error),
    68→      }),
    69→  }),
    70→)
    71→
    72→export const toUint16PublicKeyNtt = Effect.fn(
    73→  "AccountDeployPipeline.toUint16PublicKeyNtt",
    74→)((publicKeyNtt: Int32Array) =>
    75→  Effect.try({
    76→    try: () => {
    77→      if (publicKeyNtt.length !== FALCON_PUBLIC_KEY_LENGTH) {
    78→        throw new Error(
    79→          `publicKeyNtt must have ${FALCON_PUBLIC_KEY_LENGTH} coefficients`,
    80→        )
    81→      }
    82→
    83→      const packed = new Uint16Array(FALCON_PUBLIC_KEY_LENGTH)
    84→      for (let i = 0; i < publicKeyNtt.length; i += 1) {
    85→        const coeff = publicKeyNtt[i]
    86→        if (coeff < 0 || coeff >= FALCON_MODULUS) {
    87→          throw new Error(`publicKeyNtt coefficient out of range at index ${i}`)
    88→        }
    89→        packed[i] = coeff
    90→      }
    91→
    92→      return packed
    93→    },
    94→    catch: (error) =>
    95→      new InvalidPublicKeyNttError({
    96→        message: String(error),
    97→      }),
    98→  }),
    99→)
   100→
   101→interface PrepareAccountDeployInput {
   102→  readonly privateKey: string
   103→  readonly existingKeypair: Option.Option<FalconKeypair>
   104→}
   105→
   106→export const prepareAccountDeployEffect = Effect.fn(
   107→  "AccountDeployPipeline.prepareAccountDeployEffect",
   108→)(function* ({ privateKey, existingKeypair }: PrepareAccountDeployInput) {
   109→  const normalizedPrivateKey = yield* validateHexPrivateKey(privateKey)
   110→
   111→  const keypair = yield* Option.match(existingKeypair, {
   112→    onNone: () => FalconService.generateKeypair(),
   113→    onSome: (currentKeypair) => Effect.succeed(currentKeypair),
   114→  })
   115→
   116→  const publicKeyNtt = yield* toUint16PublicKeyNtt(keypair.publicKeyNtt)
   117→  const packedPublicKey = yield* FalconService.packPublicKey(publicKeyNtt)
   118→  const { address, salt } = yield* StarknetService.computeDeployAddress(packedPublicKey)
   119→
   120→  return {
   121→    privateKey: normalizedPrivateKey,
   122→    keypair,
   123→    packedPublicKey,
   124→    address,
   125→    salt,
   126→  } satisfies PreparedAccountDeploy
   127→})
   128→
   129→export const deployAccountEffect = Effect.fn(
   130→  "AccountDeployPipeline.deployAccountEffect",
   131→)(function* ({
   132→  address,
   133→  packedPublicKey,
   134→  signer,
   135→  salt,
   136→  requiredBalance,
   137→}: DeployAccountInput) {
   138→  const balance = yield* StarknetService.getBalance(address)
   139→
   140→  if (balance < requiredBalance) {
   141→    return yield* Effect.fail(
   142→      new InsufficientFundsError({
   143→        message: "Insufficient STRK balance. Fund the account and try again.",
   144→        address,
   145→        required: requiredBalance.toString(),
   146→      }),
   147→    )
   148→  }
   149→
   150→  const deployment = yield* StarknetService.deployAccount(
   151→    packedPublicKey,
   152→    signer,
   153→    salt,
   154→  )
   155→
   156→  return deployment
   157→})
   158→

1→import { Context, Effect, Layer } from "effect"
     2→import { WasmLoadError } from "./errors"
     3→
     4→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     5→// WasmModule interface
     6→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→
     8→export interface WasmModule {
     9→  readonly keygen: (seed: Uint8Array) => { sk: Uint8Array; vk: Uint8Array }
    10→  readonly sign: (
    11→    skBytes: Uint8Array,
    12→    message: Uint8Array,
    13→    salt: Uint8Array,
    14→  ) => {
    15→    signature: Uint8Array
    16→    salt: Uint8Array
    17→  }
    18→  readonly verify: (
    19→    vkBytes: Uint8Array,
    20→    message: Uint8Array,
    21→    signature: Uint8Array,
    22→  ) => boolean
    23→  readonly create_verification_hint: (
    24→    s1: Int32Array,
    25→    pkNtt: Int32Array,
    26→  ) => Uint16Array
    27→  readonly pack_public_key_wasm: (pkNtt: Uint16Array) => string[]
    28→  readonly public_key_length: () => number
    29→  readonly salt_length: () => number
    30→  readonly sign_for_starknet: (
    31→    skBytes: Uint8Array,
    32→    txHash: string,
    33→    pkNtt: Int32Array,
    34→  ) => string[]
    35→}
    36→
    37→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    38→// Runtime type validation — no `as any` or `as unknown as WasmModule`
    39→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    40→
    41→/**
    42→ * Runtime guard that checks every method in the WasmModule interface exists
    43→ * and is a function. Returns true only when all seven methods are present.
    44→ *
    45→ * This replaces the unsafe `as unknown as WasmModule` cast that was used in
    46→ * the original implementation.
    47→ *
    48→ * Validation scope: checks that each required key is present and is a
    49→ * function. Function arity and return types cannot be checked without
    50→ * invoking the function (which would have side effects), so only presence
    51→ * and kind are validated here.
    52→ *
    53→ * No type assertions are used: after the null/typeof guard, TypeScript
    54→ * narrows `mod` to `object`, which satisfies `Reflect.get`'s `target:
    55→ * object` parameter — property access is type-safe without any cast.
    56→ */
    57→export function isValidWasmModule(mod: unknown): mod is WasmModule {
    58→  if (mod === null || typeof mod !== "object") return false
    59→  // TypeScript narrows `mod` to `object` here — no type assertion needed.
    60→  // Reflect.get accepts `object` as its first argument directly.
    61→  const methods = [
    62→    "keygen",
    63→    "sign",
    64→    "verify",
    65→    "create_verification_hint",
    66→    "pack_public_key_wasm",
    67→    "public_key_length",
    68→    "salt_length",
    69→    "sign_for_starknet",
    70→  ] as const
    71→  return methods.every((method) => typeof Reflect.get(mod, method) === "function")
    72→}
    73→
    74→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75→// Context.Tag — WasmRuntime service tag
    76→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    77→
    78→export class WasmRuntime extends Context.Tag("WasmRuntime")<
    79→  WasmRuntime,
    80→  WasmModule
    81→>() {}
    82→
    83→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    84→// Internal load function — uses Effect.fn for automatic span tracing
    85→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    86→
    87→const loadWasmModule = Effect.fn("WasmRuntime.load")(function* () {
    88→  const mod = yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    89→    try: async () => {
    90→      // Dynamic import of the WASM JS glue generated by wasm-pack.
    91→      // The `/* webpackIgnore: true */` comment prevents bundlers from
    92→      // trying to resolve this path at build time.
    93→      const modulePath = "/wasm/falcon_rs.js"
    94→      const imported = await import(
    95→        /* webpackIgnore: true */
    96→        modulePath
    97→      )
    98→      // Run the WASM initializer (wasm-pack generates a default export
    99→      // that downloads and instantiates the .wasm binary).
   100→      await imported.default()
   101→      return imported
   102→    },
   103→    catch: (error) =>
   104→      new WasmLoadError({
   105→        message: `Failed to load WASM module: ${String(error)}`,
   106→      }),
   107→  })
   108→
   109→  // Runtime validation — replaces unsafe `as unknown as WasmModule`
   110→  if (!isValidWasmModule(mod)) {
   111→    return yield* Effect.fail(
   112→      new WasmLoadError({
   113→        message:
   114→          "WASM module is missing required methods. Expected: keygen, sign, verify, " +
   115→          "create_verification_hint, pack_public_key_wasm, public_key_length, salt_length, sign_for_starknet.",
   116→      }),
   117→    )
   118→  }
   119→
   120→  yield* Effect.log("WasmRuntime loaded successfully")
   121→  return mod
   122→})
   123→
   124→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   125→// WasmRuntimeLive — Layer that loads and validates the WASM module
   126→//
   127→// Effect's Layer memoization guarantees this effect runs at most once when
   128→// the Layer is provided to the application. There is no mutable global state
   129→// (`let cachedModule`); the singleton guarantee comes from Layer itself.
   130→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   131→
   132→export const WasmRuntimeLive = Layer.effect(WasmRuntime, loadWasmModule())
   133→

1→import { Effect } from "effect"
     2→import { WasmRuntime } from "./WasmRuntime"
     3→import {
     4→  KeygenError,
     5→  SigningError,
     6→  HintGenerationError,
     7→  PackingError,
     8→  VerificationError,
     9→} from "./errors"
    10→import type {
    11→  FalconKeypair,
    12→  FalconSignatureResult,
    13→  PackedPublicKey,
    14→} from "./types"
    15→
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→// VK byte deserialization
    18→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    19→
    20→/**
    21→ * Deserialize a Falcon-512 verifying key (896 bytes) into 512 NTT-domain
    22→ * coefficients stored as an Int32Array.
    23→ *
    24→ * Format (falcon-rs, PUBLIC_KEY_LEN = 896, no header):
    25→ *   - 512 coefficients x 14 bits each, packed LSB-first
    26→ *
    27→ * Algorithm: maintains a bit-buffer, loading bytes from the input one at a
    28→ * time and extracting 14-bit windows.
    29→ *
    30→ * Throws on invalid input (too few bytes, or any coefficient >= Q=12289).
    31→ */
    32→function parsePublicKeyNttBytes(vkBytes: Uint8Array): Int32Array {
    33→  const Q = 12289
    34→  const N = 512
    35→  const BITS_PER_COEFF = 14
    36→  const DATA_BYTES = (N * BITS_PER_COEFF) / 8 // 896
    37→
    38→  if (vkBytes.length < DATA_BYTES) {
    39→    throw new Error(
    40→      `VK too short: expected at least ${DATA_BYTES} bytes, got ${vkBytes.length}`,
    41→    )
    42→  }
    43→
    44→  const coeffs = new Int32Array(N)
    45→  const mask = (1 << BITS_PER_COEFF) - 1 // 0x3FFF
    46→
    47→  let bitBuffer = 0
    48→  let bitsInBuffer = 0
    49→  let byteIndex = 0
    50→
    51→  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
    52→    while (bitsInBuffer < BITS_PER_COEFF) {
    53→      bitBuffer |= vkBytes[byteIndex++] << bitsInBuffer
    54→      bitsInBuffer += 8
    55→    }
    56→    const coeff = bitBuffer & mask
    57→    bitBuffer >>= BITS_PER_COEFF
    58→    bitsInBuffer -= BITS_PER_COEFF
    59→
    60→    if (coeff >= Q) {
    61→      throw new Error(
    62→        `Invalid VK coefficient ${coeff} at index ${i} (must be < ${Q})`,
    63→      )
    64→    }
    65→    coeffs[i] = coeff
    66→  }
    67→
    68→  return coeffs
    69→}
    70→
    71→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    72→// FalconService
    73→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    74→
    75→export class FalconService extends Effect.Service<FalconService>()(
    76→  "FalconService",
    77→  {
    78→    accessors: true,
    79→    // No pre-bundled dependencies: callers must provide WasmRuntime via Layer.
    80→    // This enables unit tests to inject a mock WasmRuntime without the live
    81→    // loader trying to fetch /wasm/falcon_rs.js at import time.
    82→    effect: Effect.gen(function* () {
    83→      const wasm = yield* WasmRuntime
    84→
    85→      const generateKeypair = Effect.fn("Falcon.generateKeypair")(
    86→        function* (seed?: Uint8Array) {
    87→          const s =
    88→            seed ?? crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(32))
    89→          const result = yield* Effect.try({
    90→            try: () => wasm.keygen(s),
    91→            catch: (error) =>
    92→              new KeygenError({ message: String(error) }),
    93→          })
    94→          const publicKeyNtt = yield* Effect.try({
    95→            try: () => parsePublicKeyNttBytes(result.vk),
    96→            catch: (error) =>
    97→              new KeygenError({ message: String(error) }),
    98→          })
    99→          return {
   100→            secretKey: result.sk,
   101→            verifyingKey: result.vk,
   102→            publicKeyNtt,
   103→          } satisfies FalconKeypair
   104→        },
   105→      )
   106→
   107→      const sign = Effect.fn("Falcon.sign")(
   108→        function* (
   109→          secretKey: Uint8Array,
   110→          message: Uint8Array,
   111→        ) {
   112→          const result = yield* Effect.try({
   113→            try: () =>
   114→              wasm.sign(secretKey, message, crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(40))),
   115→            catch: (error) =>
   116→              new SigningError({ message: String(error) }),
   117→          })
   118→          return result satisfies FalconSignatureResult
   119→        },
   120→      )
   121→
   122→      const verify = Effect.fn("Falcon.verify")(
   123→        function* (
   124→          verifyingKey: Uint8Array,
   125→          message: Uint8Array,
   126→          signature: Uint8Array,
   127→        ) {
   128→          return yield* Effect.try({
   129→            try: () => wasm.verify(verifyingKey, message, signature),
   130→            catch: (error) =>
   131→              new VerificationError({
   132→                message: String(error),
   133→                step: "verify",
   134→              }),
   135→          })
   136→        },
   137→      )
   138→
   139→      const createHint = Effect.fn("Falcon.createHint")(
   140→        function* (
   141→          s1: Int32Array,
   142→          pkNtt: Int32Array,
   143→        ) {
   144→          return yield* Effect.try({
   145→            try: () => wasm.create_verification_hint(s1, pkNtt),
   146→            catch: (error) =>
   147→              new HintGenerationError({ message: String(error) }),
   148→          })
   149→        },
   150→      )
   151→
   152→      const packPublicKey = Effect.fn("Falcon.packPublicKey")(
   153→        function* (
   154→          pkNtt: Uint16Array,
   155→        ) {
   156→          const slots = yield* Effect.try({
   157→            try: () => wasm.pack_public_key_wasm(pkNtt),
   158→            catch: (error) =>
   159→              new PackingError({ message: String(error) }),
   160→          })
   161→          return { slots } satisfies PackedPublicKey
   162→        },
   163→      )
   164→
   165→      const deserializePublicKeyNtt = Effect.fn(
   166→        "Falcon.deserializePublicKeyNtt",
   167→      )(function* (vkBytes: Uint8Array) {
   168→        return yield* Effect.try({
   169→          try: () => parsePublicKeyNttBytes(vkBytes),
   170→          catch: (error) =>
   171→            new KeygenError({ message: String(error) }),
   172→        })
   173→      })
   174→
   175→      const signForStarknet = Effect.fn("Falcon.signForStarknet")(
   176→        function* (
   177→          secretKey: Uint8Array,
   178→          txHash: string,
   179→          pkNtt: Int32Array,
   180→        ) {
   181→          return yield* Effect.try({
   182→            try: () => wasm.sign_for_starknet(secretKey, txHash, pkNtt),
   183→            catch: (error) =>
   184→              new SigningError({ message: String(error) }),
   185→          })
   186→        },
   187→      )
   188→
   189→      return {
   190→        generateKeypair,
   191→        sign,
   192→        verify,
   193→        createHint,
   194→        packPublicKey,
   195→        deserializePublicKeyNtt,
   196→        signForStarknet,
   197→      }
   198→    }),
   199→  },
   200→) {}
   201→

1→import { Config, Effect, Layer } from "effect"
     2→import { RpcProvider, Account, hash, stark } from "starknet"
     3→import type { SignerInterface } from "starknet"
     4→import {
     5→  StarknetRpcError,
     6→  AccountDeployError,
     7→  DevnetFetchError,
     8→  TransactionSubmitError,
     9→} from "./errors"
    10→import { TxHash, ContractAddress } from "./types"
    11→import type { PackedPublicKey, DevnetAccount } from "./types"
    12→
    13→/** STRK token contract address (same on mainnet and Sepolia) */
    14→export const STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS =
    15→  "0x04718f5a0fc34cc1af16a1cdee98ffb20c31f5cd61d6ab07201858f4287c938d"
    16→
    17→export function parsePrefundedAccounts(raw: unknown[]): DevnetAccount[] {
    18→  return raw
    19→    .filter((item): item is Record<string, string> =>
    20→      typeof item === "object" && item !== null &&
    21→      "address" in item && "private_key" in item
    22→    )
    23→    .map((item) => ({
    24→      address: item.address,
    25→      private_key: item.private_key,
    26→      initial_balance: item.initial_balance ?? "0",
    27→    }))
    28→}
    29→
    30→function makeService(rpcUrl: string, classHash: string) {
    31→  const provider = new RpcProvider({ nodeUrl: rpcUrl })
    32→
    33→  const computeDeployAddress = Effect.fn("Starknet.computeDeployAddress")(
    34→    function* (packedPk: PackedPublicKey) {
    35→      // PackedPolynomial512 is a flat struct (s0..s28), not an Array.
    36→      // CallData.compile would add a length prefix; pass raw slots instead.
    37→      const constructorCalldata = [...packedPk.slots]
    38→      const salt = stark.randomAddress()
    39→      const address = hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(
    40→        salt,
    41→        classHash,
    42→        constructorCalldata,
    43→        0,
    44→      )
    45→      return {
    46→        address: ContractAddress.make(address),
    47→        salt,
    48→      }
    49→    },
    50→  )
    51→
    52→  const getBalance = Effect.fn("Starknet.getBalance")(
    53→    function* (address: string) {
    54→      return yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    55→        try: async () => {
    56→          const result = await provider.callContract({
    57→            contractAddress: STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS,
    58→            entrypoint: "balanceOf",
    59→            calldata: [address],
    60→          })
    61→          return BigInt(result[0])
    62→        },
    63→        catch: (error) =>
    64→          new StarknetRpcError({ message: String(error), code: -1 }),
    65→      })
    66→    },
    67→  )
    68→
    69→  const deployAccount = Effect.fn("Starknet.deployAccount")(
    70→    function* (
    71→      packedPk: PackedPublicKey,
    72→      signer: SignerInterface,
    73→      salt: string,
    74→    ) {
    75→      // PackedPolynomial512 is a flat struct (s0..s28), not an Array.
    76→      // CallData.compile would add a length prefix; pass raw slots instead.
    77→      const constructorCalldata = [...packedPk.slots]
    78→      const address = hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(
    79→        salt,
    80→        classHash,
    81→        constructorCalldata,
    82→        0,
    83→      )
    84→
    85→      const account = new Account({ provider, address, signer })
    86→
    87→      const result = yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    88→        try: () =>
    89→          account.deployAccount({
    90→            classHash,
    91→            constructorCalldata,
    92→            addressSalt: salt,
    93→            contractAddress: address,
    94→          }),
    95→        catch: (error) =>
    96→          new AccountDeployError({ message: String(error) }),
    97→      })
    98→
    99→      yield* Effect.tryPromise({
   100→        try: () => provider.waitForTransaction(result.transaction_hash),
   101→        catch: (error) =>
   102→          new AccountDeployError({
   103→            message: `Tx failed: ${error}`,
   104→            txHash: result.transaction_hash,
   105→          }),
   106→      })
   107→
   108→      return {
   109→        txHash: TxHash.make(result.transaction_hash),
   110→        address: ContractAddress.make(result.contract_address),
   111→      }
   112→    },
   113→  )
   114→
   115→  const waitForTx = Effect.fn("Starknet.waitForTx")(
   116→    function* (txHash: string) {
   117→      yield* Effect.tryPromise({
   118→        try: () => provider.waitForTransaction(txHash),
   119→        catch: (error) =>
   120→          new StarknetRpcError({ message: String(error), code: -1 }),
   121→      })
   122→    },
   123→  )
   124→
   125→  const fetchPrefundedAccounts = Effect.fn("Starknet.fetchPrefundedAccounts")(
   126→    function* () {
   127→      return yield* Effect.tryPromise({
   128→        try: async () => {
   129→          const baseUrl = rpcUrl.replace(/\/rpc.*$/, "").replace(/\/$/, "")
   130→          const response = await fetch(baseUrl, {
   131→            method: "POST",
   132→            headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
   133→            body: JSON.stringify({
   134→              jsonrpc: "2.0",
   135→              id: 1,
   136→              method: "devnet_getPredeployedAccounts",
   137→              params: [],
   138→            }),
   139→          })
   140→          if (!response.ok) {
   141→            throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}`)
   142→          }
   143→          const json = await response.json()
   144→          if (json.error) {
   145→            throw new Error(json.error.message ?? JSON.stringify(json.error))
   146→          }
   147→          return parsePrefundedAccounts(json.result as unknown[])
   148→        },
   149→        catch: (error) =>
   150→          new DevnetFetchError({ message: `Failed to fetch prefunded accounts: ${error}` }),
   151→      })
   152→    },
   153→  )
   154→
   155→  const sendTransaction = Effect.fn("Starknet.sendTransaction")(
   156→    function* (
   157→      accountAddress: string,
   158→      signer: SignerInterface,
   159→      recipient: string,
   160→      amount: bigint,
   161→    ) {
   162→      const account = new Account({ provider, address: accountAddress, signer })
   163→      return yield* Effect.tryPromise({
   164→        try: async () => {
   165→          const result = await account.execute([
   166→            {
   167→              contractAddress: STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS,
   168→              entrypoint: "transfer",
   169→              calldata: [recipient, amount.toString(), "0"],
   170→            },
   171→          ])
   172→          await provider.waitForTransaction(result.transaction_hash)
   173→          return { txHash: TxHash.make(result.transaction_hash) }
   174→        },
   175→        catch: (error) =>
   176→          new TransactionSubmitError({ message: String(error) }),
   177→      })
   178→    },
   179→  )
   180→
   181→  return {
   182→    computeDeployAddress,
   183→    getBalance,
   184→    deployAccount,
   185→    waitForTx,
   186→    fetchPrefundedAccounts,
   187→    sendTransaction,
   188→    provider,
   189→  }
   190→}
   191→
   192→// @ts-ignore — StarknetService.make() intentionally overrides the inherited Effect.Service.make() with a Layer factory
   193→export class StarknetService extends Effect.Service<StarknetService>()(
   194→  "StarknetService",
   195→  {
   196→    accessors: true,
   197→    effect: Effect.gen(function* () {
   198→      const rpcUrl = yield* Config.string("NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL")
   199→      return makeService(rpcUrl, "0x0")
   200→    }),
   201→  },
   202→) {
   203→  /**
   204→   * Custom layer factory for runtime network switching.
   205→   * Use this instead of Default when you need a specific rpcUrl and classHash
   206→   * (e.g., switching between Sepolia and Mainnet at runtime).
   207→   */
   208→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
   209→  static make(rpcUrl: string, classHash: string): Layer.Layer<StarknetService> {
   210→    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
   211→    return Layer.succeed(StarknetService, makeService(rpcUrl, classHash) as any)
   212→  }
   213→}
   214→

1→import { Atom } from "@effect-atom/atom"
     2→import { Option } from "effect"
     3→import type { FalconKeypair, FalconSignatureResult, PackedPublicKey, VerificationStep } from "../services/types"
     4→
     5→export const wasmStatusAtom = Atom.make<"loading" | "ready" | "error">("loading").pipe(
     6→  Atom.keepAlive,
     7→)
     8→
     9→export const keypairAtom = Atom.make<Option.Option<FalconKeypair>>(Option.none()).pipe(
    10→  Atom.keepAlive,
    11→)
    12→
    13→export const signatureAtom = Atom.make<Option.Option<FalconSignatureResult>>(Option.none())
    14→
    15→export const packedKeyAtom = Atom.make<Option.Option<PackedPublicKey>>(Option.none()).pipe(
    16→  Atom.keepAlive,
    17→)
    18→
    19→export const verificationStepAtom = Atom.make<VerificationStep>({ step: "idle" })
    20→
    21→export const messageAtom = Atom.make("")
    22→

1→import { Atom } from "@effect-atom/atom"
     2→import type { PipelineStep } from "../services/types"
     3→
     4→const PIPELINE_STEPS: PipelineStep[] = [
     5→  {
     6→    id: "hash-to-point",
     7→    name: "hash_to_point",
     8→    description: "Poseidon hash: message || salt → 512 Zq coefficients",
     9→    input: "message + salt",
    10→    output: "512 coefficients ∈ [0, 12289)",
    11→    stepCount: 5988,
    12→    status: "pending",
    13→  },
    14→  {
    15→    id: "ntt-s1",
    16→    name: "NTT(s1)",
    17→    description: "Forward NTT on signature polynomial s1",
    18→    input: "s1 (512 coefficients)",
    19→    output: "s1_ntt (frequency domain)",
    20→    stepCount: 15000,
    21→    status: "pending",
    22→  },
    23→  {
    24→    id: "pointwise-mul",
    25→    name: "s1_ntt * pk_ntt",
    26→    description: "Pointwise multiplication in NTT domain",
    27→    input: "s1_ntt, pk_ntt",
    28→    output: "product_ntt (512 values)",
    29→    stepCount: 1500,
    30→    status: "pending",
    31→  },
    32→  {
    33→    id: "ntt-hint",
    34→    name: "NTT(mul_hint)",
    35→    description: "Forward NTT on verification hint (provided off-chain)",
    36→    input: "mul_hint (512 coefficients)",
    37→    output: "hint_ntt — must equal product_ntt",
    38→    stepCount: 15000,
    39→    status: "pending",
    40→  },
    41→  {
    42→    id: "recover-s0",
    43→    name: "s0 = msg_point - mul_hint",
    44→    description: "Recover s0 from message point and hint",
    45→    input: "msg_point, mul_hint",
    46→    output: "s0 (512 coefficients)",
    47→    stepCount: 500,
    48→    status: "pending",
    49→  },
    50→  {
    51→    id: "norm-check",
    52→    name: "‖(s0, s1)‖² ≤ bound",
    53→    description: "Check combined Euclidean norm against security bound",
    54→    input: "s0, s1",
    55→    output: "pass/fail (bound = 34,034,726)",
    56→    stepCount: 26000,
    57→    status: "pending",
    58→  },
    59→]
    60→
    61→export const INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS = PIPELINE_STEPS
    62→
    63→export const pipelineStepsAtom = Atom.make<PipelineStep[]>(PIPELINE_STEPS)
    64→
    65→export const pipelineActiveStepAtom = Atom.make<number>(-1)
    66→
    67→export const pipelinePlayingAtom = Atom.make(false)
    68→
