content.content:
1→import { Cause, Exit, type ManagedRuntime, Option } from "effect"
     2→import {
     3→  type Call,
     4→  type InvocationsSignerDetails,
     5→  type DeployAccountSignerDetails,
     6→  type DeclareSignerDetails,
     7→  type Signature,
     8→  type TypedData,
     9→  SignerInterface,
    10→  hash,
    11→  transaction,
    12→  CallData,
    13→  EDAMode,
    14→  type EDataAvailabilityMode,
    15→  typedData as typedDataUtils,
    16→} from "starknet"
    17→import { FalconService } from "./FalconService"
    18→import { TransactionSignError } from "./errors"
    19→
    20→function intDAM(dam: EDataAvailabilityMode): EDAMode {
    21→  if (dam === "L1") return EDAMode.L1
    22→  if (dam === "L2") return EDAMode.L2
    23→  throw new Error(`Unknown EDataAvailabilityMode: ${dam}`)
    24→}
    25→
    26→export class FalconSigner extends SignerInterface {
    27→  constructor(
    28→    private readonly sk: Uint8Array,
    29→    private readonly pkNtt: Int32Array,
    30→    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    31→    private readonly runtime: ManagedRuntime.ManagedRuntime<FalconService, any>,
    32→  ) {
    33→    super()
    34→  }
    35→
    36→  async getPubKey(): Promise<string> {
    37→    // Falcon public keys are not a single felt — return a placeholder.
    38→    // The actual PK is the 29 packed felt252 slots stored in the contract.
    39→    return "0x0"
    40→  }
    41→
    42→  async signMessage(
    43→    typedDataObj: TypedData,
    44→    accountAddress: string,
    45→  ): Promise<Signature> {
    46→    const msgHash = typedDataUtils.getMessageHash(typedDataObj, accountAddress)
    47→    return this._signHash(msgHash)
    48→  }
    49→
    50→  async signTransaction(
    51→    transactions: Call[],
    52→    transactionsDetail: InvocationsSignerDetails,
    53→  ): Promise<Signature> {
    54→    const compiledCalldata = transaction.getExecuteCalldata(
    55→      transactions,
    56→      transactionsDetail.cairoVersion,
    57→    )
    58→    const txHash = hash.calculateInvokeTransactionHash({
    59→      ...transactionsDetail,
    60→      senderAddress: transactionsDetail.walletAddress,
    61→      compiledCalldata,
    62→      version: transactionsDetail.version,
    63→      nonceDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(transactionsDetail.nonceDataAvailabilityMode),
    64→      feeDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(transactionsDetail.feeDataAvailabilityMode),
    65→    })
    66→    return this._signHash(txHash)
    67→  }
    68→
    69→  async signDeployAccountTransaction(
    70→    details: DeployAccountSignerDetails,
    71→  ): Promise<Signature> {
    72→    const compiledConstructorCalldata = CallData.compile(
    73→      details.constructorCalldata,
    74→    )
    75→    const txHash = hash.calculateDeployAccountTransactionHash({
    76→      ...details,
    77→      salt: details.addressSalt,
    78→      compiledConstructorCalldata,
    79→      version: details.version,
    80→      nonceDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(details.nonceDataAvailabilityMode),
    81→      feeDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(details.feeDataAvailabilityMode),
    82→    })
    83→    return this._signHash(txHash)
    84→  }
    85→
    86→  async signDeclareTransaction(
    87→    details: DeclareSignerDetails,
    88→  ): Promise<Signature> {
    89→    const txHash = hash.calculateDeclareTransactionHash({
    90→      ...details,
    91→      version: details.version,
    92→      nonceDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(details.nonceDataAvailabilityMode),
    93→      feeDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(details.feeDataAvailabilityMode),
    94→    })
    95→    return this._signHash(txHash)
    96→  }
    97→
    98→  private async _signHash(txHash: string): Promise<string[]> {
    99→    const exit = await this.runtime.runPromiseExit(
   100→      FalconService.signForStarknet(this.sk, txHash, this.pkNtt),
   101→    )
   102→
   103→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
   104→      return exit.value
   105→    }
   106→
   107→    const errOpt = Cause.failureOption(exit.cause)
   108→    const msg = Option.match(errOpt, {
   109→      onNone: () => "Falcon signing failed",
   110→      onSome: (e) => e.message,
   111→    })
   112→    throw new TransactionSignError({ message: msg })
   113→  }
   114→}
   115→

1→[package]
     2→name = "falcon_account"
     3→version = "0.1.0"
     4→edition = "2024_07"
     5→
     6→[dependencies]
     7→starknet = "2.15.1"
     8→falcon = { path = "../falcon" }
     9→openzeppelin_interfaces = { git = "https://github.com/OpenZeppelin/cairo-contracts.git", tag = "v4.0.0-alpha.0" }
    10→openzeppelin_introspection = { git = "https://github.com/OpenZeppelin/cairo-contracts.git", tag = "v4.0.0-alpha.0" }
    11→openzeppelin_utils = { git = "https://github.com/OpenZeppelin/cairo-contracts.git", tag = "v4.0.0-alpha.0" }
    12→
    13→[dev-dependencies]
    14→snforge_std = { git = "https://github.com/foundry-rs/starknet-foundry", tag = "v0.55.0" }
    15→assert_macros = "2.15.1"
    16→
    17→[[target.starknet-contract]]
    18→allowed-libfuncs-list.name = "experimental"
    19→sierra = true
    20→casm = true
    21→
    22→[target-defaults.test]
    23→build-external-contracts = [
    24→    "falcon_account::preset::FalconAccount",
    25→]
    26→

"@effect-atom/atom-react" 6→import { FalconService } from "@/services/FalconService" 7→import { WasmRuntimeLive } from "@/services/WasmRuntime" 8→import { 9→ keypairAtom, 10→ packedKeyAtom, 11→ verificationStepAtom, 12→ wasmStatusAtom, 13→} from "@/atoms/falcon" 14→import { exportKeyFile, parseKeyFile } from "@/services/keyfile" 15→import { HexDisplay } from "./HexDisplay" 16→import { bytesToHex } from "./verification-utils" 17→ 18→const appRuntime = ManagedRuntime.make( 19→ FalconService.Default.pipe(Layer.provide(WasmRuntimeLive)), 20→) 21→ 22→export function "@effect-atom/atom-react" 6→import { FalconService } from "@/services/FalconService" 7→import { WasmRuntimeLive } from "@/services/WasmRuntime" 8→import { 9→ keypairAtom, 10→ messageAtom, 11→ packedKeyAtom, 12→ signatureAtom, 13→ verificationStepAtom, 14→} from "@/atoms/falcon" 15→import { HexDisplay } from "./HexDisplay" 16→import { bytesToHex, getVerificationDisabledState } from 40→ const kp = Option.match(keypair, { 41→ onNone: () => null, 42→ onSome: (k) => k, 43→ }) 44→ if (!kp) return 45→ 46→ const startTime = Date.now() 47→ const messageBytes = new TextEncoder().encode(message) 48→ 49→ // Sign 50→ setStep({ step: "signing" }) 51→ const signExit = await appRuntime.runPromiseExit( 52→ FalconService.sign(kp.secretKey, messageBytes), 53→ ) 54→ 55→ if (Exit.isFailure(signExit)) { 56→ const errOpt = Cause.failureOption(signExit.cause) 57→ const msg = Option.match(errOpt, { 58→ onNone: () => "Signing failed", 59→ onSome: (e) => e.message, 60→ }) 61→ setStep({ step: "error", message: msg }) 62→ return 63→ } 64→ 65→ const sigResult = signExit.value 66→ setSignature(Option.some(sigResult)) 67→ 68→ // Pack Public Key 69→ setStep({ step: "packing" }) 70→ const pkNtt16 = new Uint16Array(kp.publicKeyNtt.length) 71→ for (let i = 0; i < kp.publicKeyNtt.length; i++) { 72→ pkNtt16[i] = kp.publicKeyNtt[i] 73→ } 74→ const packExit = await appRuntime.runPromiseExit( 75→ FalconService.packPublicKey(pkNtt16), 76→ ) 77→ 78→ if (Exit.isFailure(packExit)) { 79→ const errOpt = Cause.failureOption(packExit.cause) 80→ const msg = Date.now() - startTime 107→ setStep({ step: "complete", valid: verifyExit.value, durationMs }) 108→ }, [keypair, message, setPackedKey, setSignature, setStep]) 109→ 110→ const signatureHex = Each step card shows name, description, step count, and 8→ * expands to show input/output when active. 9→ * 10→ * All state lives in atoms (pipelineStepsAtom, pipelineActiveStepAtom, 11→ * pipelinePlayingAtom) — no local useState. 12→ */ 13→ 14→import React, { useCallback, useEffect, useRef } from "react" 15→import { useAtomValue, useAtomSet } from "@effect-atom/atom-react" 16→import { 17→ pipelineStepsAtom, 18→ pipelineActiveStepAtom, 19→ pipelinePlayingAtom, 20→ INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS, 21→} from "@/atoms/pipeline" 22→import type { PipelineStep } from "@/services/types" 23→ 24→const TOTAL_STEPS = INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS.reduce( 25→ (sum, s) => sum + s.stepCount, 26→ 0, 27→) 28→ 29→export function PipelineVisualizer(): React.JSX.Element { 30→ const steps = useAtomValue(pipelineStepsAtom) 31→ const activeStep = useAtomValue(pipelineActiveStepAtom) 32→ const playing = useAtomValue(pipelinePlayingAtom) 33→ 34→ const setSteps = useAtomSet(pipelineStepsAtom) 35→ const setActiveStep = useAtomSet(pipelineActiveStepAtom) 36→ const setPlaying = useAtomSet(pipelinePlayingAtom) 37→ 38→ const intervalRef = useRef | null>(null) 39→ 40→ // ── Auto-advance logic ────────────────────────────────────────────── 41→ useEffect(() => { 42→ if (!playing) { 43→ if (intervalRef.current) clearInterval(intervalRef.current) 44→ return 45→ } 46→ 47→ intervalRef.current = setInterval(() => { 48→ setActiveStep((prev) => { 49→ const next = prev + 1 50→ if (next >= steps.length) { 51→ setPlaying(false) 52→ setSteps((s) => 53→ s.map((step, i) => 54→ i === prev ? { ...step, status: "complete" as const } : step, 55→ ), 56→ ) 57→ return prev 58→ } 59→ setSteps((s) => 60→ s.map((step, i) => { 61→ if (i === prev) return { ...step, status: "complete" as const } 62→ if (i === next) return { ...step, status: "active" as const } 63→ return step 64→ }), 65→ ) 66→ return next 67→ }) 68→ }, 2000) 69→ 70→ return () => { 71→ if (intervalRef.current) clearInterval(intervalRef.current) 72→ } 73→ }, [playing, steps.length, setActiveStep, setSteps, setPlaying]) 74→ 75→ // ── Handlers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 76→ const handlePlay = useCallback(() => { 77→ if (activeStep === -1) { 78→ setSteps((s) => 79→ s.map((step, i) => 80→ i === 0 81→ ? { ...step, status: "active" as const } 82→ : { ...step, status: "pending" as const }, 83→ ), 84→ ) 85→ setActiveStep(0) 86→ } 87→ setPlaying(true) 88→ }, [activeStep, setSteps, setActiveStep, setPlaying]) 89→ 90→ const handlePause = useCallback(() => { 91→ setPlaying(false) 92→ }, [setPlaying]) 93→ 94→ const handleStep = useCallback(() => { 95→ setPlaying(false) 96→ setActiveStep((prev) => { 97→ const next = prev + 1 98→ if (next >= steps.length) { 99→ setSteps((s) => 100→ s.map((step, i) => 101→ i === prev ? { ...step, status: "complete" as const } : step, 102→ ), 103→ ) 104→ return prev 105→ } 106→ if (prev === -1) { 107→ setSteps((s) => 108→ s.map((step, i) => 109→ i === 0 110→ ? { ...step, status: "active" as const } 111→ : { ...step, status: "pending" as const }, 112→ ), 113→ ) 114→ return 0 115→ } 116→ setSteps((s) => 117→ s.map((step, i) => { 118→ if (i === prev) return { ...step, status: "complete" as const } 119→ if (i === next) return { ...step, status: "active" as const } 120→ return step 121→ }), 122→ ) 123→ return next 124→ }) 125→ }, [steps.length, setActiveStep, setSteps, setPlaying]) 126→ 127→ const handleReset = useCallback(() => { 128→ setPlaying(false) 129→ setActiveStep(-1) 130→ setSteps(INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS) 131→ }, [setPlaying, setActiveStep, setSteps]) 132→ 133→ const allComplete = steps.every((s) => s.status === "complete") 134→ 135→ return ( 136→

     2→import type { PipelineStep } from "../services/types"
     3→
     4→const PIPELINE_STEPS: PipelineStep[] = [
     5→  {
     6→    id: "hash-to-point",
     7→    name: "hash_to_point",
     8→    description: "Poseidon hash: message || salt → 512 Zq coefficients",
     9→    input: "message + salt",
    10→    output: "512 coefficients ∈ [0, 12289)",
    11→    stepCount: 5988,
    12→    status: "pending",
    13→  },
    14→  {
    15→    id: "ntt-s1",
    16→    name: "NTT(s1)",
    17→    description: "Forward NTT on signature polynomial s1",
    18→    input: "s1 (512 coefficients)",
    19→    output: "s1_ntt (frequency domain)",
    20→    stepCount: 15000,
    21→    status: "pending",
    22→  },
    23→  {
    24→    id: "pointwise-mul",
    25→    name: "s1_ntt * pk_ntt",
    26→    description: "Pointwise multiplication in NTT domain",
    27→    input: "s1_ntt, pk_ntt",
    28→    output: "product_ntt (512 values)",
    29→    stepCount: 1500,
    30→    status: "pending",
    31→  },
    32→  {
    33→    id: "ntt-hint",
    34→    name: "NTT(mul_hint)",

    36→    input: "mul_hint (512 coefficients)",
    37→    output: "hint_ntt — must equal product_ntt",
    38→    stepCount: 15000,
    39→    status: "pending",
    40→  },
    41→  {
    42→    id: "recover-s0",

    44→    description: "Recover s0 from message point and hint",

    46→    output: "s0 (512 coefficients)",
    47→    stepCount: 500,
    48→    status: "pending",
    49→  },

    51→    id: "norm-check",

    53→    description: "Check combined Euclidean norm against security bound",
    54→    input: "s0, s1",
    55→    output: "pass/fail (bound = 34,034,726)",

1→/**
     2→ * Browser-safe verification utility functions.
     3→ *
     4→ * These utilities are designed to work in the browser without Node.js
     5→ * built-ins like `Buffer`. All hex conversions use Uint8Array directly.
     6→ */
     7→
     8→import { Option } from "effect"
     9→import type { FalconKeypair, VerificationStep } from "@/services/types"
    10→
    11→// ─── Hex conversion ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    12→
    13→/**
    14→ * Convert a Uint8Array to a lowercase hex string.
    15→ *
    16→ * Browser-safe: uses Array.from + toString(16) instead of Buffer.from.
    17→ * This works in all modern browsers and in Node.js without any imports.
    18→ */
    19→export function bytesToHex(bytes: Uint8Array): string {
    20→  return Array.from(bytes)
    21→    .map((b) => b.toString(16).padStart(2, "0"))
    22→    .join("")
    23→}
    24→
    25→/**
    26→ * Truncate a long hex or felt252 string to `<head>...<tail>` format.
    27→ *
    28→ * If the string is at most `head + tail + 3` characters (including the "..."
    29→ * separator), it is returned unchanged — truncation would not reduce length.
    30→ *
    31→ * @param value - The hex/felt string to truncate.
    32→ * @param head  - Number of characters to keep at the start.
    33→ * @param tail  - Number of characters to keep at the end.
    34→ */
    35→export function truncateHex(value: string, head: number, tail: number): string {
    36→  if (value.length <= head + tail + 3) return value
    37→  return `${value.slice(0, head)}...${value.slice(-tail)}`
    38→}
    39→
    40→// ─── Disabled-state derivation ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    41→
    42→export interface VerificationDisabledState {
    43→  /** True while any async operation (keygen / sign / verify) is in flight. */
    44→  readonly isBusy: boolean
    45→  /** True when the Generate Keypair button should be enabled. */
    46→  readonly canGenerate: boolean
    47→  /**
    48→   * True when the Sign & Verify button should be enabled.
    49→   *
    50→   * Requires:
    51→   *  - Not busy
    52→   *  - Keypair is Some
    53→   *  - Message is non-empty (after trimming)
    54→   */
    55→  readonly canSign: boolean
    56→}
    57→
    58→/** Steps that represent an in-flight async operation. */
    59→const BUSY_STEPS = new Set<VerificationStep["step"]>([
    60→  "generating-keypair",
    61→  "signing",
    62→  "creating-hint",
    63→  "packing",
    64→  "verifying",
    65→])
    66→
    67→/**
    68→ * Derive button disabled states from the current atom values.
    69→ *
    70→ * Centralised here (not inline in the component) so it can be unit-tested
    71→ * independently of React rendering.
    72→ */
    73→export function getVerificationDisabledState(args: {
    74→  keypair: Option.Option<FalconKeypair>
    75→  message: string
    76→  step: VerificationStep
    77→}): VerificationDisabledState {
    78→  const { keypair, message, step } = args
    79→
    80→  const isBusy = BUSY_STEPS.has(step.step)
    81→  const canGenerate = !isBusy
    82→  const canSign =
    83→    !isBusy && Option.isSome(keypair) && message.trim().length > 0
    84→
    85→  return { isBusy, canGenerate, canSign }
    86→}
    87→

N more" indicator. 22→ */ 23→ readonly maxRows?: number 24→ /** 25→ * If provided, each displayed value is truncated to 26→ * `head` characters + "..." + `tail` characters. 27→ */ 28→ readonly truncate?: { readonly head: number; readonly tail: number } 29→} 30→ 31→export function HexDisplay(props: HexDisplayProps): React.JSX.Element { 32→ const { label, value, maxRows, truncate } = props 33→ 34→ const display = (v: string): string => 35→ truncate ? truncateHex(v, truncate.head, truncate.tail) : v 36→ 37→ if (typeof value === "string") { 38→ return ( 39→

58→ 59→ {label} 60→ 61→
62→ {shown.map((v) => { 63→ const nextCount = (keyCounts.get(v) ?? 0) + 1 64→ keyCounts.set(v, nextCount) 65→ const stableKey = nextCount === 1 ? v : `${v}-${nextCount}` 66→ return ( 67→ 71→ {display(v)} 72→ 73→ ) 74→ })} 75→ {overflow > 0 && ( 76→ 77→ ... {overflow} more 78→ 79→ )} 80→

content.content:
1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→set -euo pipefail
     3→
     4→# Declare FalconAccount on a Starknet network
     5→# Usage: ./bin/declare.sh devnet|sepolia|mainnet [--account <name>]
     6→
     7→NETWORK="${1:?Usage: ./bin/declare.sh devnet|sepolia|mainnet}"
     8→shift
     9→
    10→case "$NETWORK" in
    11→  devnet|sepolia|mainnet) ;;
    12→  *) echo "ERROR: Unknown network '$NETWORK'. Use: devnet, sepolia, or mainnet"; exit 1 ;;
    13→esac
    14→
    15→SIERRA_PATH="target/dev/falcon_account_FalconAccount.contract_class.json"
    16→CASM_PATH="target/dev/falcon_account_FalconAccount.compiled_contract_class.json"
    17→
    18→if [[ ! -f "$SIERRA_PATH" ]]; then
    19→  echo "ERROR: Sierra artifact not found at $SIERRA_PATH"
    20→  echo "Run: scarb build --package falcon_account"
    21→  exit 1
    22→fi
    23→
    24→if [[ ! -f "$CASM_PATH" ]]; then
    25→  echo "ERROR: CASM artifact not found at $CASM_PATH"
    26→  echo "Ensure casm = true in packages/falcon_account/Scarb.toml and rebuild"
    27→  exit 1
    28→fi
    29→
    30→echo "Declaring FalconAccount on $NETWORK..."
    31→
    32→OUTPUT=$(sncast --profile "$NETWORK" declare \
    33→  --contract-name FalconAccount \
    34→  --package falcon_account \
    35→  "$@" 2>&1) || true
    36→
    37→echo "$OUTPUT"
    38→
    39→# Extract class_hash from sncast output
    40→CLASS_HASH=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -oP 'class_hash: \K0x[0-9a-fA-F]+' || true)
    41→
    42→if [[ -z "$CLASS_HASH" ]]; then
    43→  # Already declared — extract from "is already declared" message
    44→  CLASS_HASH=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -oP '0x[0-9a-fA-F]{50,}' | head -1 || true)
    45→fi
    46→
    47→if [[ -n "$CLASS_HASH" ]]; then
    48→  echo ""
    49→  echo "Class hash: $CLASS_HASH"
    50→
    51→  # Auto-patch networks.ts
    52→  NETWORKS_FILE="apps/demo/src/config/networks.ts"
    53→  if [[ -f "$NETWORKS_FILE" ]]; then
    54→    # Find the network block and replace its classHash
    55→    # Uses perl for multi-line matching
    56→    perl -i -0pe '
    57→      s/(id: "'"$NETWORK"'".*?classHash: ")0x0(")/${1}'"${CLASS_HASH}"'${2}/s
    58→    ' "$NETWORKS_FILE"
    59→    echo "Updated $NETWORKS_FILE with classHash for $NETWORK"
    60→  fi
    61→else
    62→  echo ""
    63→  echo "Could not extract class_hash. Update networks.ts manually."
    64→fi
    65→

1→"use client"
     2→
     3→import { useEffect, useState } from "react"
     4→
     5→export type Theme = "dark" | "light"
     6→
     7→/** Design-system color tokens for each theme (mirrors globals.css) */
     8→export const THEME_TOKENS = {
     9→  dark: {
    10→    "falcon-bg": "#0f172a",
    11→    "falcon-surface": "#1e293b",
    12→    "falcon-text": "#f8fafc",
    13→    "falcon-muted": "#94a3b8",
    14→  },
    15→  light: {
    16→    "falcon-bg": "#ffffff",
    17→    "falcon-surface": "#f1f5f9",
    18→    "falcon-text": "#0f172a",
    19→    "falcon-muted": "#64748b",
    20→  },
    21→} as const satisfies Record<Theme, Record<string, string>>
    22→
    23→/** Brand colors — same across light and dark */
    24→export const BRAND_TOKENS = {
    25→  "falcon-primary": "#6366f1",
    26→  "falcon-secondary": "#8b5cf6",
    27→  "falcon-accent": "#06b6d4",
    28→  "falcon-success": "#10b981",
    29→  "falcon-error": "#ef4444",
    30→} as const satisfies Record<string, string>
    31→
    32→/**
    33→ * ThemeToggle — floating button that switches the <html> className
    34→ * between "dark" and "light", triggering Tailwind's class-based dark mode.
    35→ *
    36→ * Defaults to dark (matching the SSR layout.tsx className="dark").
    37→ */
    38→export function ThemeToggle() {
    39→  const [theme, setTheme] = useState<Theme>("dark")
    40→
    41→  useEffect(() => {
    42→    document.documentElement.className = theme
    43→  }, [theme])
    44→
    45→  const toggle = () => setTheme((prev) => (prev === "dark" ? "light" : "dark"))
    46→
    47→  return (
    48→    <button
    49→      onClick={toggle}
    50→      className="fixed top-4 right-4 z-50 rounded-lg border border-falcon-muted/20 bg-falcon-surface p-2 text-sm hover:bg-falcon-surface/80 transition-colors"
    51→      aria-label={theme === "dark" ? "Switch to light mode" : "Switch to dark mode"}
    52→      data-testid="theme-toggle"
    53→      data-theme={theme}
    54→    >
    55→      {theme === "dark" ? "☀️" : "🌙"}
    56→    </button>
    57→  )
    58→}
    59→

1→"use client"
     2→
     3→import React, { useCallback, useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useState } from "react"
     4→import { useAtomSet, useAtomValue } from "@effect-atom/atom-react"
     5→import { Cause, Exit, Layer, ManagedRuntime, Option } from "effect"
     6→import { keypairAtom } from "@/atoms/falcon"
     7→import {
     8→  deployStepAtom,
     9→  deployedAddressAtom,
    10→  deployTxHashAtom,
    11→  networkAtom,
    12→} from "@/atoms/starknet"
    13→import { NETWORKS } from "@/config/networks"
    14→import type { NetworkConfig } from "@/config/networks"
    15→import { FalconService } from "@/services/FalconService"
    16→import { FalconSigner } from "@/services/FalconSigner"
    17→import { StarknetService } from "@/services/StarknetService"
    18→import { WasmRuntimeLive } from "@/services/WasmRuntime"
    19→import type { DevnetAccount } from "@/services/types"
    20→import { SendTransaction } from "./SendTransaction"
    21→import type { PreparedAccountDeploy } from "./accountDeployPipeline"
    22→import {
    23→  deployAccountEffect,
    24→  prepareAccountDeployEffect,
    25→} from "./accountDeployPipeline"
    26→
    27→const falconRuntime = ManagedRuntime.make(
    28→  FalconService.Default.pipe(Layer.provide(WasmRuntimeLive)),
    29→)
    30→
    31→function createDeployRuntime(config: NetworkConfig) {
    32→  return ManagedRuntime.make(
    33→    Layer.mergeAll(
    34→      FalconService.Default.pipe(Layer.provide(WasmRuntimeLive)),
    35→      StarknetService.make(config.rpcUrl, config.classHash),
    36→    ),
    37→  )
    38→}
    39→
    40→const extractFailureMessage = <A, E extends { readonly message: string }>(
    41→  exit: Exit.Exit<A, E>,
    42→  fallback: string,
    43→): string => {
    44→  if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
    45→    return fallback
    46→  }
    47→  const failure = Cause.failureOption(exit.cause)
    48→  return Option.match(failure, {
    49→    onNone: () => fallback,
    50→    onSome: (error) => error.message,
    51→  })
    52→}
    53→
    54→export function AccountDeployFlow(): React.JSX.Element {
    55→  const keypair = useAtomValue(keypairAtom)
    56→  const deployStep = useAtomValue(deployStepAtom)
    57→  const setKeypair = useAtomSet(keypairAtom)
    58→  const setDeployStep = useAtomSet(deployStepAtom)
    59→  const setDeployedAddress = useAtomSet(deployedAddressAtom)
    60→  const setDeployTxHash = useAtomSet(deployTxHashAtom)

content.text:
I now have a complete picture of the entire demo app. Here is the full analysis.

---

## Demo App Architecture: Complete Analysis

### 1. Overall App Structure

The app is a single-page Next.js 15 application (App Router, React 19, Tailwind CSS 4). The single route is `src/app/page.tsx`, which stacks sections vertically. There are no nested routes or API routes.

**Page layout (top to bottom):**

```
NavHeader         — sticky top bar with network switcher (devnet/sepolia/mainnet)
Hero              — headline, CTA buttons, stat chips (63K steps / 62 felts / 29 slots)
WhyPostQuantum    — four info cards: Quantum Threat, Account Abstraction, Falcon-512, Hints
PerformanceStats  — benchmark table + calldata efficiency callout
PlaygroundSection — dynamic import (ssr:false) → VerificationPlayground
PipelineSection   — dynamic import (ssr:false) → PipelineVisualizer
AccountDeploySection — dynamic import (ssr:false) → AccountDeployFlow
Footer            — GitHub + Starknet docs links
```

**Key files by category:**

| Category | Files |
|---|---|
| App shell | `src/app/layout.tsx`, `src/app/page.tsx`, `src/app/providers.tsx` |
| Landing | `components/landing/{Hero,NavHeader,PerformanceStats,WhyPostQuantum,Footer}.tsx` |
| Interactive | `components/interactive/{VerificationPlayground,KeyManagementPanel,SignVerifyPanel,PipelineVisualizer,AccountDeployFlow,SendTransaction}.tsx` |
| Section wrappers | `components/interactive/{PlaygroundSection,PipelineSection,AccountDeploySection}.tsx` |
| Services | `services/{FalconService,FalconSigner,StarknetService,WasmRuntime,keyfile,errors,types}.ts` |
| Atoms (state) | `atoms/{falcon,starknet,pipeline}.ts` |
| Config | `config/networks.ts` |

**State management** uses `@effect-atom/atom` + `@effect-atom/atom-react` (Effect-TS-native atoms), wrapped by a `RegistryProvider` in `providers.tsx`. Effect's `ManagedRuntime` is used to run Effect programs from React event handlers.

---

### 2. Deployment Flow: Account Deploy on Devnet

The flow is orchestrated by `AccountDeployFlow.tsx` and the pipeline logic in `accountDeployPipeline.ts`. The `DeployStep` union type in `atoms/starknet.ts` tracks the machine state:

```
idle → generating-keypair → packing → computing-address → awaiting-funds → deploying → deployed
```

**Step 1: Prepare (button "Prepare Deploy")**

`handlePrepare` runs `prepareAccountDeployEffect` which chains:

1. `validateHexPrivateKey(privateKey)` — normalizes the deployer key to a 32-byte hex string
2. If no keypair exists: `FalconService.generateKeypair()` (calls WASM `keygen`)  
   Otherwise: reuses the existing keypair from `keypairAtom`
3. `toUint16PublicKeyNtt(keypair.publicKeyNtt)` — converts Int32Array → Uint16Array, validates [0, 12289)
4. `FalconService.packPublicKey(pkNtt16)` — calls WASM `pack_public_key_wasm`, yields 29 hex felt252 strings
5. `StarknetService.computeDeployAddress(packedPublicKey)` — computes counterfactual address using `hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(salt, classHash, [...packedPk.slots], 0)` with a random salt

The result (`PreparedAccountDeploy`) holds `{keypair, packedPublicKey, address, salt}`.

The UI then transitions to `awaiting-funds`, showing the computed address with a copy button and a link to the Starknet Faucet (for non-devnet). On devnet, a balance poller fires every 5 seconds via `StarknetService.getBalance(address)`.

**Devnet prefunding:** On devnet, the component auto-fetches prefunded accounts via a `devnet_getPredeployedAccounts` JSON-RPC call to `http://localhost:5050`, then pre-populates the "Deployer Account" dropdown with them.

**Step 2: Deploy (button "Deploy Account")**

`handleDeploy` runs `deployAccountEffect` which:

1. Checks balance >= 1 wei (fails with `InsufficientFundsError` if not)

```typescript
// 2. Instantiates a FalconSigner with the keypair
const signer = new FalconSigner(kp.secretKey, kp.publicKeyNtt, falconRuntime)

// 3. Converts amount string → bigint wei (1e18 scaling)
const amountWei = BigInt(Math.floor(parseFloat(amount) * 1e18))

// 4. Runs StarknetService.sendTransaction

  recipient,         // default = deployedAddress (self-send demo)
```

- Creates `new Account({ provider, address: accountAddress, signer })`
- Calls `account.execute([{ contractAddress: STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS, entrypoint: "transfer", calldata: [recipient, amount.toString(), "0"] }])`
- Waits for transaction confirmation
- Returns `{ txHash }`

The STRK token address is hardcoded: `0x04718f5a0fc34cc1af16a1cdee98ffb20c31f5cd61d6ab07201858f4287c938d`.

|---|---|

```typescript
private async _signHash(txHash: string): Promise<string[]> {
  const exit = await this.runtime.runPromiseExit(
    FalconService.signForStarknet(this.sk, txHash, this.pkNtt),
  )
  // ...returns string[] on success, throws TransactionSignError on failure
}
```

```
mainnet → https://api.zan.top/...mainnet classHash: 0x0        isDevnet: false
```

```typescript

sign(sk, message, salt): { signature: Uint8Array; salt: Uint8Array }

create_verification_hint(s1: Int32Array, pkNtt: Int32Array): Uint16Array
```


## Demo App Architecture: Complete Analysis

### 1. Overall App Structure

The app is a single-page Next.js 15 application (App Router, React 19, Tailwind CSS 4). The single route is `src/app/page.tsx`, which stacks sections vertically. There are no nested routes or API routes.

**Page layout (top to bottom):**

```
NavHeader         — sticky top bar with network switcher (devnet/sepolia/mainnet)
Hero              — headline, CTA buttons, stat chips (63K steps / 62 felts / 29 slots)
WhyPostQuantum    — four info cards: Quantum Threat, Account Abstraction, Falcon-512, Hints
PerformanceStats  — benchmark table + calldata efficiency callout
PlaygroundSection — dynamic import (ssr:false) → VerificationPlayground
PipelineSection   — dynamic import (ssr:false) → PipelineVisualizer
AccountDeploySection — dynamic import (ssr:false) → AccountDeployFlow
Footer            — GitHub + Starknet docs links
```

**Key files by category:**

| Category | Files |
|---|---|
| App shell | `src/app/layout.tsx`, `src/app/page.tsx`, `src/app/providers.tsx` |
| Landing | `components/landing/{Hero,NavHeader,PerformanceStats,WhyPostQuantum,Footer}.tsx` |
| Interactive | `components/interactive/{VerificationPlayground,KeyManagementPanel,SignVerifyPanel,PipelineVisualizer,AccountDeployFlow,SendTransaction}.tsx` |
| Section wrappers | `components/interactive/{PlaygroundSection,PipelineSection,AccountDeploySection}.tsx` |
| Services | `services/{FalconService,FalconSigner,StarknetService,WasmRuntime,keyfile,errors,types}.ts` |
| Atoms (state) | `atoms/{falcon,starknet,pipeline}.ts` |
| Config | `config/networks.ts` |

**State management** uses `@effect-atom/atom` + `@effect-atom/atom-react` (Effect-TS-native atoms), wrapped by a `RegistryProvider` in `providers.tsx`. Effect's `ManagedRuntime` is used to run Effect programs from React event handlers.

---

### 2. Deployment Flow: Account Deploy on Devnet

The flow is orchestrated by `AccountDeployFlow.tsx` and the pipeline logic in `accountDeployPipeline.ts`. The `DeployStep` union type in `atoms/starknet.ts` tracks the machine state:

```
idle → generating-keypair → packing → computing-address → awaiting-funds → deploying → deployed
```

**Step 1: Prepare (button "Prepare Deploy")**

`handlePrepare` runs `prepareAccountDeployEffect` which chains:

1. `validateHexPrivateKey(privateKey)` — normalizes the deployer key to a 32-byte hex string
2. If no keypair exists: `FalconService.generateKeypair()` (calls WASM `keygen`)  
   Otherwise: reuses the existing keypair from `keypairAtom`
3. `toUint16PublicKeyNtt(keypair.publicKeyNtt)` — converts Int32Array → Uint16Array, validates [0, 12289)
4. `FalconService.packPublicKey(pkNtt16)` — calls WASM `pack_public_key_wasm`, yields 29 hex felt252 strings
5. `StarknetService.computeDeployAddress(packedPublicKey)` — computes counterfactual address using `hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(salt, classHash, [...packedPk.slots], 0)` with a random salt

The result (`PreparedAccountDeploy`) holds `{keypair, packedPublicKey, address, salt}`.

The UI then transitions to `awaiting-funds`, showing the computed address with a copy button and a link to the Starknet Faucet (for non-devnet). On devnet, a balance poller fires every 5 seconds via `StarknetService.getBalance(address)`.

**Devnet prefunding:** On devnet, the component auto-fetches prefunded accounts via a `devnet_getPredeployedAccounts` JSON-RPC call to `http://localhost:5050`, then pre-populates the "Deployer Account" dropdown with them.

**Step 2: Deploy (button "Deploy Account")**

`handleDeploy` runs `deployAccountEffect` which:

1. Checks balance >= 1 wei (fails with `InsufficientFundsError` if not)
2. Creates a `FalconSigner(secretKey, publicKeyNtt, falconRuntime)`
3. Calls `StarknetService.deployAccount(packedPublicKey, signer, salt)` which:
   - Recomputes `constructorCalldata = [...packedPk.slots]` (29 felts, no length prefix)
   - Recomputes `address` from hash
   - Constructs starknet.js `Account({ provider, address, signer })`
   - Calls `account.deployAccount({ classHash, constructorCalldata, addressSalt, contractAddress })`
   - Waits for transaction confirmation via `provider.waitForTransaction`

**Class hash** (`0x37c7626b50ba517835c9556b53bce57cb6633704bb702f8c2f13a44cf151d5`) is the same on both devnet and Sepolia (hardcoded in `networks.ts`). Mainnet uses `"0x0"` (not yet declared), which shows a yellow warning banner.


- `pack_public_key_wasm(pkNtt)` - packs 512 Zq values into 29 felt252 strings
- `public_key_length()` / `salt_length()` - constants
- `sign_for_starknet(skBytes, txHash, pkNtt)` - returns calldata string array

```json
}
```

1→import { Effect, Option, Schema } from "effect"
     2→import type { SignerInterface } from "starknet"
     3→import { FalconService } from "@/services/FalconService"
     4→import { StarknetService } from "@/services/StarknetService"
     5→import { InsufficientFundsError } from "@/services/errors"
     6→import type {
     7→  ContractAddress,
     8→  FalconKeypair,
     9→  PackedPublicKey,
    10→} from "@/services/types"
    11→
    12→const FALCON_PUBLIC_KEY_LENGTH = 512
    13→const FALCON_MODULUS = 12289
    14→
    15→export class InvalidPrivateKeyError extends Schema.TaggedError<InvalidPrivateKeyError>()(
    16→  "InvalidPrivateKeyError",
    17→  { message: Schema.String },
    18→) {}
    19→
    20→export class InvalidPublicKeyNttError extends Schema.TaggedError<InvalidPublicKeyNttError>()(
    21→  "InvalidPublicKeyNttError",
    22→  { message: Schema.String },
    23→) {}
    24→
    25→export interface PreparedAccountDeploy {
    26→  readonly privateKey: string
    27→  readonly keypair: FalconKeypair
    28→  readonly packedPublicKey: PackedPublicKey
    29→  readonly address: ContractAddress
    30→  readonly salt: string
    31→}
    32→
    33→export interface DeployAccountInput {
    34→  readonly address: ContractAddress
    35→  readonly packedPublicKey: PackedPublicKey
    36→  readonly signer: SignerInterface
    37→  readonly salt: string
    38→  readonly requiredBalance: bigint
    39→}
    40→
    41→export const validateHexPrivateKey = Effect.fn(
    42→  "AccountDeployPipeline.validateHexPrivateKey",
    43→)((privateKey: string) =>
    44→  Effect.try({
    45→    try: () => {
    46→      const trimmed = privateKey.trim()
    47→      const normalized = trimmed.toLowerCase()
    48→
    49→      if (!normalized.startsWith("0x")) {
    50→        throw new Error("Private key must be 0x-prefixed")
    51→      }
    52→
    53→      const hexBody = normalized.slice(2)
    54→      if (hexBody.length === 0 || hexBody.length > 64) {
    55→        throw new Error("Private key must be between 1 and 32 bytes hex")
    56→      }
    57→
    58→      if (!/^[0-9a-f]+$/u.test(hexBody)) {
    59→        throw new Error("Private key contains non-hex characters")
    60→      }
    61→
    62→      // Pad to 32 bytes for consistency (Starknet private keys may be shorter)
    63→      return `0x${hexBody.padStart(64, "0")}`
    64→    },
    65→    catch: (error) =>
    66→      new InvalidPrivateKeyError({
    67→        message: String(error),
    68→      }),
    69→  }),
    70→)
    71→
    72→export const toUint16PublicKeyNtt = Effect.fn(
    73→  "AccountDeployPipeline.toUint16PublicKeyNtt",
    74→)((publicKeyNtt: Int32Array) =>
    75→  Effect.try({
    76→    try: () => {
    77→      if (publicKeyNtt.length !== FALCON_PUBLIC_KEY_LENGTH) {
    78→        throw new Error(
    79→          `publicKeyNtt must have ${FALCON_PUBLIC_KEY_LENGTH} coefficients`,
    80→        )
    81→      }
    82→
    83→      const packed = new Uint16Array(FALCON_PUBLIC_KEY_LENGTH)
    84→      for (let i = 0; i < publicKeyNtt.length; i += 1) {
    85→        const coeff = publicKeyNtt[i]
    86→        if (coeff < 0 || coeff >= FALCON_MODULUS) {
    87→          throw new Error(`publicKeyNtt coefficient out of range at index ${i}`)
    88→        }
    89→        packed[i] = coeff
    90→      }
    91→
    92→      return packed
    93→    },
    94→    catch: (error) =>
    95→      new InvalidPublicKeyNttError({
    96→        message: String(error),
    97→      }),
    98→  }),
    99→)
   100→
   101→interface PrepareAccountDeployInput {
   102→  readonly privateKey: string
   103→  readonly existingKeypair: Option.Option<FalconKeypair>
   104→}
   105→
   106→export const prepareAccountDeployEffect = Effect.fn(
   107→  "AccountDeployPipeline.prepareAccountDeployEffect",
   108→)(function* ({ privateKey, existingKeypair }: PrepareAccountDeployInput) {
   109→  const normalizedPrivateKey = yield* validateHexPrivateKey(privateKey)
   110→
   111→  const keypair = yield* Option.match(existingKeypair, {
   112→    onNone: () => FalconService.generateKeypair(),
   113→    onSome: (currentKeypair) => Effect.succeed(currentKeypair),
   114→  })
   115→
   116→  const publicKeyNtt = yield* toUint16PublicKeyNtt(keypair.publicKeyNtt)
   117→  const packedPublicKey = yield* FalconService.packPublicKey(publicKeyNtt)
   118→  const { address, salt } = yield* StarknetService.computeDeployAddress(packedPublicKey)
   119→
   120→  return {
   121→    privateKey: normalizedPrivateKey,
   122→    keypair,
   123→    packedPublicKey,
   124→    address,
   125→    salt,
   126→  } satisfies PreparedAccountDeploy
   127→})
   128→
   129→export const deployAccountEffect = Effect.fn(
   130→  "AccountDeployPipeline.deployAccountEffect",
   131→)(function* ({
   132→  address,
   133→  packedPublicKey,
   134→  signer,
   135→  salt,
   136→  requiredBalance,
   137→}: DeployAccountInput) {
   138→  const balance = yield* StarknetService.getBalance(address)
   139→
   140→  if (balance < requiredBalance) {
   141→    return yield* Effect.fail(
   142→      new InsufficientFundsError({
   143→        message: "Insufficient STRK balance. Fund the account and try again.",
   144→        address,
   145→        required: requiredBalance.toString(),
   146→      }),
   147→    )
   148→  }
   149→
   150→  const deployment = yield* StarknetService.deployAccount(
   151→    packedPublicKey,
   152→    signer,
   153→    salt,
   154→  )
   155→
   156→  return deployment
   157→})
   158→

1→import { Config, Effect, Layer } from "effect"
     2→import { RpcProvider, Account, hash, stark } from "starknet"
     3→import type { SignerInterface } from "starknet"
     4→import {
     5→  StarknetRpcError,
     6→  AccountDeployError,
     7→  DevnetFetchError,
     8→  TransactionSubmitError,
     9→} from "./errors"
    10→import { TxHash, ContractAddress } from "./types"
    11→import type { PackedPublicKey, DevnetAccount } from "./types"
    12→
    13→/** STRK token contract address (same on mainnet and Sepolia) */
    14→export const STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS =
    15→  "0x04718f5a0fc34cc1af16a1cdee98ffb20c31f5cd61d6ab07201858f4287c938d"
    16→
    17→export function parsePrefundedAccounts(raw: unknown[]): DevnetAccount[] {
    18→  return raw
    19→    .filter((item): item is Record<string, string> =>
    20→      typeof item === "object" && item !== null &&
    21→      "address" in item && "private_key" in item
    22→    )
    23→    .map((item) => ({
    24→      address: item.address,
    25→      private_key: item.private_key,
    26→      initial_balance: item.initial_balance ?? "0",
    27→    }))
    28→}
    29→
    30→function makeService(rpcUrl: string, classHash: string) {
    31→  const provider = new RpcProvider({ nodeUrl: rpcUrl })
    32→
    33→  const computeDeployAddress = Effect.fn("Starknet.computeDeployAddress")(
    34→    function* (packedPk: PackedPublicKey) {
    35→      // PackedPolynomial512 is a flat struct (s0..s28), not an Array.
    36→      // CallData.compile would add a length prefix; pass raw slots instead.
    37→      const constructorCalldata = [...packedPk.slots]
    38→      const salt = stark.randomAddress()
    39→      const address = hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(
    40→        salt,
    41→        classHash,
    42→        constructorCalldata,
    43→        0,
    44→      )
    45→      return {
    46→        address: ContractAddress.make(address),
    47→        salt,
    48→      }
    49→    },
    50→  )
    51→
    52→  const getBalance = Effect.fn("Starknet.getBalance")(
    53→    function* (address: string) {
    54→      return yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    55→        try: async () => {
    56→          const result = await provider.callContract({
    57→            contractAddress: STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS,
    58→            entrypoint: "balanceOf",
    59→            calldata: [address],
    60→          })
    61→          return BigInt(result[0])
    62→        },
    63→        catch: (error) =>
    64→          new StarknetRpcError({ message: String(error), code: -1 }),
    65→      })
    66→    },
    67→  )
    68→
    69→  const deployAccount = Effect.fn("Starknet.deployAccount")(
    70→    function* (
    71→      packedPk: PackedPublicKey,
    72→      signer: SignerInterface,
    73→      salt: string,
    74→    ) {
    75→      // PackedPolynomial512 is a flat struct (s0..s28), not an Array.
    76→      // CallData.compile would add a length prefix; pass raw slots instead.
    77→      const constructorCalldata = [...packedPk.slots]
    78→      const address = hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(
    79→        salt,
    80→        classHash,
    81→        constructorCalldata,
    82→        0,
    83→      )
    84→
    85→      const account = new Account({ provider, address, signer })
    86→
    87→      const result = yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    88→        try: () =>
    89→          account.deployAccount({
    90→            classHash,
    91→            constructorCalldata,
    92→            addressSalt: salt,
    93→            contractAddress: address,
    94→          }),
    95→        catch: (error) =>
    96→          new AccountDeployError({ message: String(error) }),
    97→      })
    98→
    99→      yield* Effect.tryPromise({
   100→        try: () => provider.waitForTransaction(result.transaction_hash),
   101→        catch: (error) =>
   102→          new AccountDeployError({
   103→            message: `Tx failed: ${error}`,
   104→            txHash: result.transaction_hash,
   105→          }),
   106→      })
   107→
   108→      return {
   109→        txHash: TxHash.make(result.transaction_hash),
   110→        address: ContractAddress.make(result.contract_address),
   111→      }
   112→    },
   113→  )
   114→
   115→  const waitForTx = Effect.fn("Starknet.waitForTx")(
   116→    function* (txHash: string) {
   117→      yield* Effect.tryPromise({
   118→        try: () => provider.waitForTransaction(txHash),
   119→        catch: (error) =>
   120→          new StarknetRpcError({ message: String(error), code: -1 }),
   121→      })
   122→    },
   123→  )
   124→
   125→  const fetchPrefundedAccounts = Effect.fn("Starknet.fetchPrefundedAccounts")(
   126→    function* () {
   127→      return yield* Effect.tryPromise({
   128→        try: async () => {
   129→          const baseUrl = rpcUrl.replace(/\/rpc.*$/, "").replace(/\/$/, "")
   130→          const response = await fetch(baseUrl, {
   131→            method: "POST",
   132→            headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
   133→            body: JSON.stringify({
   134→              jsonrpc: "2.0",
   135→              id: 1,
   136→              method: "devnet_getPredeployedAccounts",
   137→              params: [],
   138→            }),
   139→          })
   140→          if (!response.ok) {
   141→            throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}`)
   142→          }
   143→          const json = await response.json()
   144→          if (json.error) {
   145→            throw new Error(json.error.message ?? JSON.stringify(json.error))
   146→          }
   147→          return parsePrefundedAccounts(json.result as unknown[])
   148→        },
   149→        catch: (error) =>
   150→          new DevnetFetchError({ message: `Failed to fetch prefunded accounts: ${error}` }),
   151→      })
   152→    },
   153→  )
   154→
   155→  const sendTransaction = Effect.fn("Starknet.sendTransaction")(
   156→    function* (
   157→      accountAddress: string,
   158→      signer: SignerInterface,
   159→      recipient: string,
   160→      amount: bigint,
   161→    ) {
   162→      const account = new Account({ provider, address: accountAddress, signer })
   163→      return yield* Effect.tryPromise({
   164→        try: async () => {
   165→          const result = await account.execute([
   166→            {
   167→              contractAddress: STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS,
   168→              entrypoint: "transfer",
   169→              calldata: [recipient, amount.toString(), "0"],
   170→            },
   171→          ])
   172→          await provider.waitForTransaction(result.transaction_hash)
   173→          return { txHash: TxHash.make(result.transaction_hash) }
   174→        },
   175→        catch: (error) =>
   176→          new TransactionSubmitError({ message: String(error) }),
   177→      })
   178→    },
   179→  )
   180→
   181→  return {
   182→    computeDeployAddress,
   183→    getBalance,
   184→    deployAccount,
   185→    waitForTx,
   186→    fetchPrefundedAccounts,
   187→    sendTransaction,
   188→    provider,
   189→  }
   190→}
   191→
   192→// @ts-ignore — StarknetService.make() intentionally overrides the inherited Effect.Service.make() with a Layer factory
   193→export class StarknetService extends Effect.Service<StarknetService>()(
   194→  "StarknetService",
   195→  {
   196→    accessors: true,
   197→    effect: Effect.gen(function* () {
   198→      const rpcUrl = yield* Config.string("NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL")
   199→      return makeService(rpcUrl, "0x0")
   200→    }),
   201→  },
   202→) {
   203→  /**
   204→   * Custom layer factory for runtime network switching.
   205→   * Use this instead of Default when you need a specific rpcUrl and classHash
   206→   * (e.g., switching between Sepolia and Mainnet at runtime).
   207→   */
   208→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
   209→  static make(rpcUrl: string, classHash: string): Layer.Layer<StarknetService> {
   210→    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
   211→    return Layer.succeed(StarknetService, makeService(rpcUrl, classHash) as any)
   212→  }
   213→}
   214→

1→import { Cause, Exit, type ManagedRuntime, Option } from "effect"
     2→import {
     3→  type Call,
     4→  type InvocationsSignerDetails,
     5→  type DeployAccountSignerDetails,
     6→  type DeclareSignerDetails,
     7→  type Signature,
     8→  type TypedData,
     9→  SignerInterface,
    10→  hash,
    11→  transaction,
    12→  CallData,
    13→  EDAMode,
    14→  type EDataAvailabilityMode,
    15→  typedData as typedDataUtils,
    16→} from "starknet"
    17→import { FalconService } from "./FalconService"
    18→import { TransactionSignError } from "./errors"
    19→
    20→function intDAM(dam: EDataAvailabilityMode): EDAMode {
    21→  if (dam === "L1") return EDAMode.L1
    22→  if (dam === "L2") return EDAMode.L2
    23→  throw new Error(`Unknown EDataAvailabilityMode: ${dam}`)
    24→}
    25→
    26→export class FalconSigner extends SignerInterface {
    27→  constructor(
    28→    private readonly sk: Uint8Array,
    29→    private readonly pkNtt: Int32Array,
    30→    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    31→    private readonly runtime: ManagedRuntime.ManagedRuntime<FalconService, any>,
    32→  ) {
    33→    super()
    34→  }
    35→
    36→  async getPubKey(): Promise<string> {
    37→    // Falcon public keys are not a single felt — return a placeholder.
    38→    // The actual PK is the 29 packed felt252 slots stored in the contract.
    39→    return "0x0"
    40→  }
    41→
    42→  async signMessage(
    43→    typedDataObj: TypedData,
    44→    accountAddress: string,
    45→  ): Promise<Signature> {
    46→    const msgHash = typedDataUtils.getMessageHash(typedDataObj, accountAddress)
    47→    return this._signHash(msgHash)
    48→  }
    49→
    50→  async signTransaction(
    51→    transactions: Call[],
    52→    transactionsDetail: InvocationsSignerDetails,
    53→  ): Promise<Signature> {
    54→    const compiledCalldata = transaction.getExecuteCalldata(
    55→      transactions,
    56→      transactionsDetail.cairoVersion,
    57→    )
    58→    const txHash = hash.calculateInvokeTransactionHash({
    59→      ...transactionsDetail,
    60→      senderAddress: transactionsDetail.walletAddress,
    61→      compiledCalldata,
    62→      version: transactionsDetail.version,
    63→      nonceDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(transactionsDetail.nonceDataAvailabilityMode),
    64→      feeDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(transactionsDetail.feeDataAvailabilityMode),
    65→    })
    66→    return this._signHash(txHash)
    67→  }
    68→
    69→  async signDeployAccountTransaction(
    70→    details: DeployAccountSignerDetails,
    71→  ): Promise<Signature> {
    72→    const compiledConstructorCalldata = CallData.compile(
    73→      details.constructorCalldata,
    74→    )
    75→    const txHash = hash.calculateDeployAccountTransactionHash({
    76→      ...details,
    77→      salt: details.addressSalt,
    78→      compiledConstructorCalldata,
    79→      version: details.version,
    80→      nonceDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(details.nonceDataAvailabilityMode),
    81→      feeDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(details.feeDataAvailabilityMode),
    82→    })
    83→    return this._signHash(txHash)
    84→  }
    85→
    86→  async signDeclareTransaction(
    87→    details: DeclareSignerDetails,
    88→  ): Promise<Signature> {
    89→    const txHash = hash.calculateDeclareTransactionHash({
    90→      ...details,
    91→      version: details.version,
    92→      nonceDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(details.nonceDataAvailabilityMode),
    93→      feeDataAvailabilityMode: intDAM(details.feeDataAvailabilityMode),
    94→    })
    95→    return this._signHash(txHash)
    96→  }
    97→
    98→  private async _signHash(txHash: string): Promise<string[]> {
    99→    const exit = await this.runtime.runPromiseExit(
   100→      FalconService.signForStarknet(this.sk, txHash, this.pkNtt),
   101→    )
   102→
   103→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
   104→      return exit.value
   105→    }
   106→
   107→    const errOpt = Cause.failureOption(exit.cause)
   108→    const msg = Option.match(errOpt, {
   109→      onNone: () => "Falcon signing failed",
   110→      onSome: (e) => e.message,
   111→    })
   112→    throw new TransactionSignError({ message: msg })
   113→  }
   114→}
   115→

1→import { Effect } from "effect"
     2→import { WasmRuntime } from "./WasmRuntime"
     3→import {
     4→  KeygenError,
     5→  SigningError,
     6→  HintGenerationError,
     7→  PackingError,
     8→  VerificationError,
     9→} from "./errors"
    10→import type {
    11→  FalconKeypair,
    12→  FalconSignatureResult,
    13→  PackedPublicKey,
    14→} from "./types"
    15→
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→// VK byte deserialization
    18→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    19→
    20→/**
    21→ * Deserialize a Falcon-512 verifying key (896 bytes) into 512 NTT-domain
    22→ * coefficients stored as an Int32Array.
    23→ *
    24→ * Format (falcon-rs, PUBLIC_KEY_LEN = 896, no header):
    25→ *   - 512 coefficients x 14 bits each, packed LSB-first
    26→ *
    27→ * Algorithm: maintains a bit-buffer, loading bytes from the input one at a
    28→ * time and extracting 14-bit windows.
    29→ *
    30→ * Throws on invalid input (too few bytes, or any coefficient >= Q=12289).
    31→ */
    32→function parsePublicKeyNttBytes(vkBytes: Uint8Array): Int32Array {
    33→  const Q = 12289
    34→  const N = 512
    35→  const BITS_PER_COEFF = 14
    36→  const DATA_BYTES = (N * BITS_PER_COEFF) / 8 // 896
    37→
    38→  if (vkBytes.length < DATA_BYTES) {
    39→    throw new Error(
    40→      `VK too short: expected at least ${DATA_BYTES} bytes, got ${vkBytes.length}`,
    41→    )
    42→  }
    43→
    44→  const coeffs = new Int32Array(N)
    45→  const mask = (1 << BITS_PER_COEFF) - 1 // 0x3FFF
    46→
    47→  let bitBuffer = 0
    48→  let bitsInBuffer = 0
    49→  let byteIndex = 0
    50→
    51→  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
    52→    while (bitsInBuffer < BITS_PER_COEFF) {
    53→      bitBuffer |= vkBytes[byteIndex++] << bitsInBuffer
    54→      bitsInBuffer += 8
    55→    }
    56→    const coeff = bitBuffer & mask
    57→    bitBuffer >>= BITS_PER_COEFF
    58→    bitsInBuffer -= BITS_PER_COEFF
    59→
    60→    if (coeff >= Q) {
    61→      throw new Error(
    62→        `Invalid VK coefficient ${coeff} at index ${i} (must be < ${Q})`,
    63→      )
    64→    }
    65→    coeffs[i] = coeff
    66→  }
    67→
    68→  return coeffs
    69→}
    70→
    71→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    72→// FalconService
    73→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    74→
    75→export class FalconService extends Effect.Service<FalconService>()(
    76→  "FalconService",
    77→  {
    78→    accessors: true,
    79→    // No pre-bundled dependencies: callers must provide WasmRuntime via Layer.
    80→    // This enables unit tests to inject a mock WasmRuntime without the live
    81→    // loader trying to fetch /wasm/falcon_rs.js at import time.
    82→    effect: Effect.gen(function* () {
    83→      const wasm = yield* WasmRuntime
    84→
    85→      const generateKeypair = Effect.fn("Falcon.generateKeypair")(
    86→        function* (seed?: Uint8Array) {
    87→          const s =
    88→            seed ?? crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(32))
    89→          const result = yield* Effect.try({
    90→            try: () => wasm.keygen(s),
    91→            catch: (error) =>
    92→              new KeygenError({ message: String(error) }),
    93→          })
    94→          const publicKeyNtt = yield* Effect.try({
    95→            try: () => parsePublicKeyNttBytes(result.vk),
    96→            catch: (error) =>
    97→              new KeygenError({ message: String(error) }),
    98→          })
    99→          return {
   100→            secretKey: result.sk,
   101→            verifyingKey: result.vk,
   102→            publicKeyNtt,
   103→          } satisfies FalconKeypair
   104→        },
   105→      )
   106→
   107→      const sign = Effect.fn("Falcon.sign")(
   108→        function* (
   109→          secretKey: Uint8Array,
   110→          message: Uint8Array,
   111→        ) {
   112→          const result = yield* Effect.try({
   113→            try: () =>
   114→              wasm.sign(secretKey, message, crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(40))),
   115→            catch: (error) =>
   116→              new SigningError({ message: String(error) }),
   117→          })
   118→          return result satisfies FalconSignatureResult
   119→        },
   120→      )
   121→
   122→      const verify = Effect.fn("Falcon.verify")(
   123→        function* (
   124→          verifyingKey: Uint8Array,
   125→          message: Uint8Array,
   126→          signature: Uint8Array,
   127→        ) {
   128→          return yield* Effect.try({
   129→            try: () => wasm.verify(verifyingKey, message, signature),
   130→            catch: (error) =>
   131→              new VerificationError({
   132→                message: String(error),
   133→                step: "verify",
   134→              }),
   135→          })
   136→        },
   137→      )
   138→
   139→      const createHint = Effect.fn("Falcon.createHint")(
   140→        function* (
   141→          s1: Int32Array,
   142→          pkNtt: Int32Array,
   143→        ) {
   144→          return yield* Effect.try({
   145→            try: () => wasm.create_verification_hint(s1, pkNtt),
   146→            catch: (error) =>
   147→              new HintGenerationError({ message: String(error) }),
   148→          })
   149→        },
   150→      )
   151→
   152→      const packPublicKey = Effect.fn("Falcon.packPublicKey")(
   153→        function* (
   154→          pkNtt: Uint16Array,
   155→        ) {
   156→          const slots = yield* Effect.try({
   157→            try: () => wasm.pack_public_key_wasm(pkNtt),
   158→            catch: (error) =>
   159→              new PackingError({ message: String(error) }),
   160→          })
   161→          return { slots } satisfies PackedPublicKey
   162→        },
   163→      )
   164→
   165→      const deserializePublicKeyNtt = Effect.fn(
   166→        "Falcon.deserializePublicKeyNtt",
   167→      )(function* (vkBytes: Uint8Array) {
   168→        return yield* Effect.try({
   169→          try: () => parsePublicKeyNttBytes(vkBytes),
   170→          catch: (error) =>
   171→            new KeygenError({ message: String(error) }),
   172→        })
   173→      })
   174→
   175→      const signForStarknet = Effect.fn("Falcon.signForStarknet")(
   176→        function* (
   177→          secretKey: Uint8Array,
   178→          txHash: string,
   179→          pkNtt: Int32Array,
   180→        ) {
   181→          return yield* Effect.try({
   182→            try: () => wasm.sign_for_starknet(secretKey, txHash, pkNtt),
   183→            catch: (error) =>
   184→              new SigningError({ message: String(error) }),
   185→          })
   186→        },
   187→      )
   188→
   189→      return {
   190→        generateKeypair,
   191→        sign,
   192→        verify,
   193→        createHint,
   194→        packPublicKey,
   195→        deserializePublicKeyNtt,
   196→        signForStarknet,
   197→      }
   198→    }),
   199→  },
   200→) {}
   201→

1→import { Context, Effect, Layer } from "effect"
     2→import { WasmLoadError } from "./errors"
     3→
     4→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     5→// WasmModule interface
     6→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→
     8→export interface WasmModule {
     9→  readonly keygen: (seed: Uint8Array) => { sk: Uint8Array; vk: Uint8Array }
    10→  readonly sign: (
    11→    skBytes: Uint8Array,
    12→    message: Uint8Array,
    13→    salt: Uint8Array,
    14→  ) => {
    15→    signature: Uint8Array
    16→    salt: Uint8Array
    17→  }
    18→  readonly verify: (
    19→    vkBytes: Uint8Array,
    20→    message: Uint8Array,
    21→    signature: Uint8Array,
    22→  ) => boolean
    23→  readonly create_verification_hint: (
    24→    s1: Int32Array,
    25→    pkNtt: Int32Array,
    26→  ) => Uint16Array
    27→  readonly pack_public_key_wasm: (pkNtt: Uint16Array) => string[]
    28→  readonly public_key_length: () => number
    29→  readonly salt_length: () => number
    30→  readonly sign_for_starknet: (
    31→    skBytes: Uint8Array,
    32→    txHash: string,
    33→    pkNtt: Int32Array,
    34→  ) => string[]
    35→}
    36→
    37→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    38→// Runtime type validation — no `as any` or `as unknown as WasmModule`
    39→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    40→
    41→/**
    42→ * Runtime guard that checks every method in the WasmModule interface exists
    43→ * and is a function. Returns true only when all seven methods are present.
    44→ *
    45→ * This replaces the unsafe `as unknown as WasmModule` cast that was used in
    46→ * the original implementation.
    47→ *
    48→ * Validation scope: checks that each required key is present and is a
    49→ * function. Function arity and return types cannot be checked without
    50→ * invoking the function (which would have side effects), so only presence
    51→ * and kind are validated here.
    52→ *
    53→ * No type assertions are used: after the null/typeof guard, TypeScript
    54→ * narrows `mod` to `object`, which satisfies `Reflect.get`'s `target:
    55→ * object` parameter — property access is type-safe without any cast.
    56→ */
    57→export function isValidWasmModule(mod: unknown): mod is WasmModule {
    58→  if (mod === null || typeof mod !== "object") return false
    59→  // TypeScript narrows `mod` to `object` here — no type assertion needed.
    60→  // Reflect.get accepts `object` as its first argument directly.
    61→  const methods = [
    62→    "keygen",
    63→    "sign",
    64→    "verify",
    65→    "create_verification_hint",
    66→    "pack_public_key_wasm",
    67→    "public_key_length",
    68→    "salt_length",
    69→    "sign_for_starknet",
    70→  ] as const
    71→  return methods.every((method) => typeof Reflect.get(mod, method) === "function")
    72→}
    73→
    74→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75→// Context.Tag — WasmRuntime service tag
    76→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    77→
    78→export class WasmRuntime extends Context.Tag("WasmRuntime")<
    79→  WasmRuntime,
    80→  WasmModule
    81→>() {}
    82→
    83→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    84→// Internal load function — uses Effect.fn for automatic span tracing
    85→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    86→
    87→const loadWasmModule = Effect.fn("WasmRuntime.load")(function* () {
    88→  const mod = yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    89→    try: async () => {
    90→      // Dynamic import of the WASM JS glue generated by wasm-pack.
    91→      // The `/* webpackIgnore: true */` comment prevents bundlers from
    92→      // trying to resolve this path at build time.
    93→      const modulePath = "/wasm/falcon_rs.js"
    94→      const imported = await import(
    95→        /* webpackIgnore: true */
    96→        modulePath
    97→      )
    98→      // Run the WASM initializer (wasm-pack generates a default export
    99→      // that downloads and instantiates the .wasm binary).
   100→      await imported.default()
   101→      return imported
   102→    },
   103→    catch: (error) =>
   104→      new WasmLoadError({
   105→        message: `Failed to load WASM module: ${String(error)}`,
   106→      }),
   107→  })
   108→
   109→  // Runtime validation — replaces unsafe `as unknown as WasmModule`
   110→  if (!isValidWasmModule(mod)) {
   111→    return yield* Effect.fail(
   112→      new WasmLoadError({
   113→        message:
   114→          "WASM module is missing required methods. Expected: keygen, sign, verify, " +
   115→          "create_verification_hint, pack_public_key_wasm, public_key_length, salt_length, sign_for_starknet.",
   116→      }),
   117→    )
   118→  }
   119→
   120→  yield* Effect.log("WasmRuntime loaded successfully")
   121→  return mod
   122→})
   123→
   124→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   125→// WasmRuntimeLive — Layer that loads and validates the WASM module
   126→//
   127→// Effect's Layer memoization guarantees this effect runs at most once when
   128→// the Layer is provided to the application. There is no mutable global state
   129→// (`let cachedModule`); the singleton guarantee comes from Layer itself.
   130→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   131→
   132→export const WasmRuntimeLive = Layer.effect(WasmRuntime, loadWasmModule())
   133→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→set -euo pipefail
     3→
     4→# Declare FalconAccount on a Starknet network
     5→# Usage: ./bin/declare.sh devnet|sepolia|mainnet [--account <name>]
     6→
     7→NETWORK="${1:?Usage: ./bin/declare.sh devnet|sepolia|mainnet}"
     8→shift
     9→
    10→case "$NETWORK" in
    11→  devnet|sepolia|mainnet) ;;
    12→  *) echo "ERROR: Unknown network '$NETWORK'. Use: devnet, sepolia, or mainnet"; exit 1 ;;
    13→esac
    14→
    15→SIERRA_PATH="target/dev/falcon_account_FalconAccount.contract_class.json"
    16→CASM_PATH="target/dev/falcon_account_FalconAccount.compiled_contract_class.json"
    17→
    18→if [[ ! -f "$SIERRA_PATH" ]]; then
    19→  echo "ERROR: Sierra artifact not found at $SIERRA_PATH"
    20→  echo "Run: scarb build --package falcon_account"
    21→  exit 1
    22→fi
    23→
    24→if [[ ! -f "$CASM_PATH" ]]; then
    25→  echo "ERROR: CASM artifact not found at $CASM_PATH"
    26→  echo "Ensure casm = true in packages/falcon_account/Scarb.toml and rebuild"
    27→  exit 1
    28→fi
    29→
    30→echo "Declaring FalconAccount on $NETWORK..."
    31→
    32→OUTPUT=$(sncast --profile "$NETWORK" declare \
    33→  --contract-name FalconAccount \
    34→  --package falcon_account \
    35→  "$@" 2>&1) || true
    36→
    37→echo "$OUTPUT"
    38→
    39→# Extract class_hash from sncast output
    40→CLASS_HASH=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -oP 'class_hash: \K0x[0-9a-fA-F]+' || true)
    41→
    42→if [[ -z "$CLASS_HASH" ]]; then
    43→  # Already declared — extract from "is already declared" message
    44→  CLASS_HASH=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -oP '0x[0-9a-fA-F]{50,}' | head -1 || true)
    45→fi
    46→

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Declare FalconAccount on a Starknet network
# Usage: ./bin/declare.sh devnet|sepolia|mainnet [--account <name>]

NETWORK="${1:?Usage: ./bin/declare.sh devnet|sepolia|mainnet}"
shift

case "$NETWORK" in
  devnet|sepolia|mainnet) ;;
  *) echo "ERROR: Unknown network '$NETWORK'. Use: devnet, sepolia, or mainnet"; exit 1 ;;
esac

SIERRA_PATH="target/dev/falcon_account_FalconAccount.contract_class.json"
CASM_PATH="target/dev/falcon_account_FalconAccount.compiled_contract_class.json"

if [[ ! -f "$SIERRA_PATH" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: Sierra artifact not found at $SIERRA_PATH"
  echo "Run: scarb build --package falcon_account"
  exit 1
fi

if [[ ! -f "$CASM_PATH" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: CASM artifact not found at $CASM_PATH"
  echo "Ensure casm = true in packages/falcon_account/Scarb.toml and rebuild"
  exit 1
fi

echo "Declaring FalconAccount on $NETWORK..."

OUTPUT=$(sncast --profile "$NETWORK" declare \
  --contract-name FalconAccount \
  --package falcon_account \
  "$@" 2>&1) || true

echo "$OUTPUT"

# Extract class_hash from sncast output
CLASS_HASH=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -oP 'class_hash: \K0x[0-9a-fA-F]+' || true)

if [[ -z "$CLASS_HASH" ]]; then
  # Already declared — extract from "is already declared" message
  CLASS_HASH=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -oP '0x[0-9a-fA-F]{50,}' | head -1 || true)
fi

if [[ -n "$CLASS_HASH" ]]; then
  echo ""
  echo "Class hash: $CLASS_HASH"

  # Auto-patch networks.ts
  NETWORKS_FILE="apps/demo/src/config/networks.ts"
  if [[ -f "$NETWORKS_FILE" ]]; then
    # Find the network block and replace its classHash
    # Uses perl for multi-line matching
    perl -i -0pe '
      s/(id: "'"$NETWORK"'".*?classHash: ")0x0(")/${1}'"${CLASS_HASH}"'${2}/s
    ' "$NETWORKS_FILE"
    echo "Updated $NETWORKS_FILE with classHash for $NETWORK"
  fi
else
  echo ""
  echo "Could not extract class_hash. Update networks.ts manually."
fi

content.thinking:
Now I have a thorough understanding of the entire codebase. Let me now write the plan. The approach is:

1. Start the demo app (it's not running)
2. Navigate to it in the browser with surf
3. Walk through the full flow:
   a. Ensure devnet is selected
   b. Click "Prepare Deploy"
   c. Wait for address to be computed
   d. Fund the account using devnet's predeployed accounts (via a manual starknet transfer from command line or via the app UI)
   e. Click "Deploy Account"
   f. After successful deploy, send STRK from the deployed Falcon account


4. Fix any issues encountered along the way

I need to clarify how the funding mechanism works on devnet—the prefunded accounts in the dropdown are used to sign the deploy transaction itself, but I need to verify whether the computed address gets funded separately or if the deployment process handles that automatically.

Looking at the actual flow in the code, I see that `deployAccount` uses the universal deployer pattern where the account deploys itself. The deployer private key validates and generates the Falcon keypair to compute the address, then in the deployment step it checks the balance of that computed address and creates a FalconSigner to execute the deployment.

So the deployer key isn't actually used for signing — it's just for validation and keypair generation. The real question is how the account gets funded in the first place, since the UI shows an "awaiting-funds" state but there's no built-in mechanism to actually send funds to the computed address on devnet. be done with:
- `starknet-devnet` has a `devnet_mint` endpoint that can mint ETH/STRK to an address

Actually, looking at starknet-devnet docs, there should be a `/mint` endpoint or similar.

But wait - the deployer private key IS being stored in `PreparedAccountDeploy`:
```
return {
    privateKey: normalizedPrivateKey,
    keypair,
    packedPublicKey,
    address,
    salt,
  }
```

But it's not used in the deploy step! The deploy step only uses `keypair`, `packedPublicKey`, `address`, `salt` and creates a FalconSigner from the keypair.

So the "deployer private key" seems vestigial or intended for funding. But it's not wired up. On devnet, we'd need to fund the account manually.

Looking at the UI flow, when a user selects a prefunded devnet account from the dropdown and clicks "Prepare Deploy", that private key gets validated and stored but never actually used for funding. The missing piece is that after we compute the address, we need to fund it—either through devnet's mint endpoint or by transferring STRK from a prefunded account. The demo app flow would be: select a deployer account, prepare the deploy to get the computed address, then fund that address before actually deploying. Defining the mint endpoint structure...

# Plan: Test & Fix Demo App E2E Flow on Devnet

## Context
The demo app (`apps/demo/`) provides a web UI for deploying a Falcon post-quantum account on Starknet and sending STRK from it. The full flow needs to be tested on devnet (`localhost:5050`) and any issues fixed along the way.

**Current state:**
- Devnet is running at `localhost:5050` with 10 prefunded accounts
- FalconAccount class hash `0x37c76...` is declared on devnet
- Demo app is **NOT running** (port 3737 is down)
- WASM artifacts exist in `apps/demo/public/wasm/`

## Steps

### 1. Start the demo dev server
- Run `cd apps/demo && npx next dev --turbopack -H 0.0.0.0 -p 3737` in background
- Wait for the server to be ready

### 2. Navigate to the app with surf and verify initial load
- `surf navigate "http://localhost:3737"`
- Take screenshot to verify the page renders
- Scroll to the "Account Deploy Flow" section

### 3. Test the Prepare Deploy flow
- Verify the devnet deployer dropdown is populated with prefunded accounts
- Click "Prepare Deploy" button
- Monitor for errors — check console for WASM loading issues, RPC errors, etc.
- Verify the address is computed and displayed in "awaiting-funds" state

### 4. Fund the computed address
- Use devnet's mint endpoint to fund the address: `POST http://localhost:5050/mint` with `{"address":"0x...","amount":10000000000000000000,"unit":"FRI"}` (STRK)
- Also mint ETH for gas: `POST http://localhost:5050/mint` with `{"address":"0x...","amount":10000000000000000000,"unit":"WEI"}`
- Verify the balance poller picks up the balance in the UI

### 5. Deploy the account
- Click "Deploy Account" button
- Monitor for errors — check console for signing issues, RPC failures
- Verify the "Account Deployed" success message with tx hash and address

### 6. Send STRK from the deployed account
- In the SendTransaction component, set a recipient (a prefunded devnet account address)
- Set amount to 0.001 STRK
- Click "Send STRK"
- Verify "Transaction Confirmed" message appears with tx hash

### 7. Fix issues along the way
- At each step, if errors occur: check browser console, network requests, and source code
- Fix any bugs in the TypeScript/React code
- Common potential issues:
  - WASM loading failures (path/CORS issues)
  - starknet.js API mismatches (v9.x breaking changes)
  - RPC errors from devnet (wrong method names, missing params)
  - Signing errors (FalconSigner integration)
  - Transaction formatting issues (calldata, fee estimation)

## Key files
- `apps/demo/src/components/interactive/AccountDeployFlow.tsx` — deploy UI
- `apps/demo/src/components/interactive/SendTransaction.tsx` — transfer UI
- `apps/demo/src/components/interactive/accountDeployPipeline.ts` — deploy logic
- `apps/demo/src/services/StarknetService.ts` — RPC interactions
- `apps/demo/src/services/FalconSigner.ts` — signing integration
- `apps/demo/src/services/FalconService.ts` — WASM bridge
- `apps/demo/src/services/WasmRuntime.ts` — WASM loader
- `apps/demo/src/config/networks.ts` — network config

## Verification
- Full E2E: page loads → prepare deploy → fund → deploy account → send STRK → confirmed
- No console errors at any stage
- Both deploy tx and transfer tx confirmed on devnet
